Japanese: 同日　午前１１時３４分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: March 21, 11:34 AM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: とりあえず、王都楼　真悟の 依頼を受けることはできた。
English: Well, at least we were able to get Mr. Matt Engarde as our client.

Japanese: そして、彼が無実であることも わかった。‥‥重要なことだ。
English: And we know that he didn't do it... Which is very important...

Japanese: つ、次は！ 次は、どうすればいいのですか！
English: So! So now what should we do!?

Japanese: ‥‥審理は明日。 チャンスは一度しかない。
English: Well, the trial is tomorrow. And we only get this one chance.

Japanese: 王都楼さんの無実を証明する カクジツな方法は、ひとつ。
English: There's only one way to prove Mr. Engarde's innocence.

Japanese: ‥‥真犯人を探し出すことだね。
English: We have to find the real killer.

Japanese: わかりました！ 探し出しましょうッ！
English: OK! Then let's start looking!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。わたくし、 お水をあげますね！
English: Oh! I'll water it!

Japanese: い、いや。もう ぼくがあげたから‥‥
English: Ah, it's OK... I already did that...

Japanese: ‥‥じょうろ、じょうろ‥‥
English: ...The watering can... Where are you, Mr. Watering Can...?

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、何かしてないと 不安なんだ‥‥）
English: (Pearls gets too wound up if she's not doing something to distract herself...)

Japanese: （チャーリーくんには、 ちょっとガマンしてもらうか）
English: (I hope Charley can withstand Typhoon Pearls...)

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: あ。何かホーリツをお調べに なるのですか！
English: Oh! Do you need to look something up in one of these law books, Mr. Nick!?

Japanese: ど、どの本が！ どの本がヒツヨウなのですかッ！
English: Wh-Which book is it!? Which book do you need!?

Japanese: ううう‥‥漢字が読めないし、 背もとどきません‥‥
English: Umm... I can't read those hard books, and... um... I can't reach them either...

Japanese: い、いいよ。 気をつかわなくて。
English: I-It's OK, Pearls. Really.

Japanese: 窓の向かいがわには、 巨大なビルがそびえている。
English: There is a giant building just outside the window.

Japanese: “ホテル・バンドー” ゴウカな高級ホテルだ。
English: It's the Gatewater Hotel, a high-class, luxury hotel.

Japanese: ちなみに、事件があったホテルは 最近、街の中心に完成した、
English: Actually, the crime happened at the recently completed hotel in the center of town,

Japanese: ホテル・バンドー・インペリアル。
English: the Gatewater Imperial Hotel.

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、どこまで デカくなっていくのやら‥‥。
English: ...Just how far are they going to expand their empire...?

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: My desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 いやにキチンとかたづいている。
English: Since I don't have time to sit, it's unusually clean...

Japanese: あ。わたくしがゆうべ、 さらにキチンとかたづけました！
English: Oh! I cleaned it up some more for you last night!

Japanese: ‥‥どうしても眠れなくて‥‥
English: ...Because I couldn't sleep...

Japanese: これは‥‥とのさまん・ほい ‥‥ですね。
English: This is the Nickel Samurai... right?

Japanese: う、うん。《ほい》だね。
English: Y-Yeah, that's right.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: 真宵さまの《ほい》に なってくださいね！
English: Please take care of Mystic Maya, and be her "Nickel Samurai", alright!?

Japanese: サイコ・ロックが 現れなかった、ってコトは‥‥
English: Since there were no Psyche- Locks,

Japanese: オートロさんは無実、 と考えていいんだよね。
English: then that means we can trust Mr. Engarde to be innocent.

Japanese: ‥‥わたくし、本当に 安心しました。
English: I really feel much better knowing that.

Japanese: もし、あのかたがハンニン だったら‥‥
English: Ever since last night, I was so worried that I kept wondering...

Japanese: ゆうべから、そればかり 考えていましたから。
English: "What if he is the real killer...? What then...?"

Japanese: ねえ。ちょっと 聞きたいことがあるんだけど。
English: Hey, I have something I want to ask you, Pearls.

Japanese: はい。なんでしょうか。
English: OK, what is it?

Japanese: どうして、ぼくと真宵ちゃんの ことばかり気にしているの？
English: Why are you so interested in how Maya and I get along?

Japanese: ぼくと真宵ちゃんは、 ベツにそのお‥‥
English: I mean, she and I don't have anything special go--

Japanese: え！　な、なんですか！
English: Huh!? Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: わた、わたくし、 なるほどくんと真宵さまは‥‥
English: B-But I thought... I thought you and Mystic Maya were...

Japanese: ちち、ちがうのですか！ まさか、そんなッ‥‥！
English: I-I've been mistaken all this time!? No way...!

Japanese: （うわわわ、このあわてぶり、 シャレにならないぞ‥‥）
English: (Aaaah! She really wasn't kidding! Pearls actually believes Maya and I are...)

Japanese: （‥‥そっとしておいた ほうがいいみたいだな‥‥）
English: (...Maybe I should've just left it alone...)

Japanese: ‥‥もう！　わるい冗談は やめてください！
English: Mr. Nick! Stop it! Don't hide your true feelings from me!

Japanese: （‥‥ビンタされた‥‥）
English: (Ow! She slapped me!!)

Japanese: ‥‥それが、ベンゴシさんの 秘宝なのですね。
English: That's a lawyer's Sacred Treasure, isn't it?

Japanese: はい？
English: I guess?

Japanese: きっと、わたくしどもの 勾玉のようなフシギなチカラが‥‥
English: I feel like it has a mysterious power, just like my Magatama...

Japanese: （うーん。こういう場合、ユメを こわさないほうがいいのか？）
English: (Hmm, well, I guess I can let her think what she wants on this one...)

Japanese: あの。わたくし、お役に 立ちたいのはやまやまなのですが。
English: Um, there are still so many things in this world that I don't understand yet.

Japanese: ‥‥修行が足りません。まだ。
English: ...Honestly, I still don't have enough training.

Japanese: い、いいよ。心当たりが ないだけなんだから。
English: Ah, no, it's OK. I don't have any ideas myself...

Japanese: すみません、すみません！
English: I'm sorry. I'm really sorry!

Japanese: ああ‥‥真宵さま。 ごブジでしょうか‥‥
English: Oh, Mystic Maya... I wonder if you're alright...

Japanese: だいじょうぶだよ。 あと１日のシンボウだから。
English: I'm sure she's OK. We have to be patient for just one more day.

Japanese: かならず、助ける。
English: I'm sure we can save her. Trust me.

Japanese: わたくしにできることでしたら、 なんでも言ってくださいね！
English: If there's anything I can do, please tell me, OK!?

Japanese: わたくしも、真宵さまのように 髪を長くしたいのですが、
English: To tell you the truth, I really want to grow my hair out, just like Mystic Maya.

Japanese: おかあさまが ゆるしてくれないのです。
English: But my mother wouldn't let me.

Japanese: でも、その髪型も にあってるよ。
English: I think your hair now suits you perfectly.

Japanese: そそ、そうですか。 ああ、ありがとうございます。
English: R-Really? Th-Thank you!

Japanese: ‥‥もじもじ。
English: *fidget, fidget*

Japanese: ‥‥もじもじ。
English: *fidget, fidget*

Japanese: あの刑事さんなら、 きっと味方になってくれます！
English: I'm sure that Mr. Detective would be our ally if we asked!

Japanese: うん。そうだね。
English: Yeah, I think so too.

Japanese: （思い返してみると、 けっこう長いつきあいだな）
English: (Looking back, we've known each other for a while now...)

Japanese: （たよりないような、 たよりになるような‥‥）
English: (He's always been trustworthy... A bit "off", but still trustworthy.)

Japanese: （フシギな刑事さんだ）
English: (What a paradox.)

Japanese: とてもやさしい目をした おじさんですよね。
English: He's a really kind and nice old man, isn't he?

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、スルドく 見抜くんだよな、ヒトを）
English: (Pearls is really perceptive when it comes to people...)

Japanese: （“おじさん”てのが 気になるけど‥‥）
English: (But what does she mean by "old man"...?)

Japanese: 春美ちゃん、ぼくは‥‥？
English: Hey, Pearls? What about me...?

Japanese: なんですか？　なるほどくん。
English: What about you, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （ダメだ。 コワくて聞けない‥‥）
English: (On second thought, I don't think I want to hear this...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: March 21 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: ああ、ベンゴシさん。 何かわかりましたか？
English: Ah, the lawyer dude. So what did you find out?

Japanese: え、ええ。まあ‥‥ ちょっと、ようすをうかがいに。
English: Um, well... I'm still in the middle of investigating...

Japanese: そうですか。
English: I see.

Japanese: ‥‥ボクのほうは、もう お話しできるコト、ないけど。
English: ...But I've already told you everything I know, dude.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 何か言いたげに こちらをジッと見ている。
English: He's so quiet, you could forget he was even there... If he wasn't watching everything.

Japanese: ‥‥うしろにかくしてるのは‥‥ 色紙とサインペンのようだ。
English: He's pushed up against the wall... Kinda like a magnet on a refrigerator.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: オートロさんがたまに チラリと視線を送っている。
English: Mr. Engarde keeps sneaking glances up at the security camera on the wall...

Japanese: 芸能人の悲しいサガ みたいなものなのかな。
English: I wonder if he is still trying to be a star?

Japanese: きのうのことを 聞かせてもらえますか？
English: Can you tell me about your activities last night?

Japanese: 賞をもらったあとは‥‥ 控え室で休んでいましたよ。
English: After I got the award, I took a break and went back to my room.

Japanese: 受賞記念のアトラクション・ ショーがありましたから。
English: I had that post-ceremony stage show to do.

Japanese: トノサマンの衣装を着たままで。
English: So I was in my Nickel Samurai costume.

Japanese: ずっと、ひとりだったんですか？
English: And you were alone the entire time?

Japanese: マネージャーさんも いそがしそうだったから。
English: My manager was running around being busy, so yeah.

Japanese: ショーのあとに、記者会見も あったはずですけど？
English: Because of the press conference you were supposed to have after the show?

Japanese: だから、知りませんよ。ボク。
English: I told you, dude. I have no idea about any press conference, alright?

Japanese: （おかしいな‥‥。トノサマンが 何か告白する、って‥‥）
English: (That's strange... I thought the Nickel Samurai was going to confess something...)

Japanese: とにかく、控え室を出たら、 なんかさわがしくて‥‥
English: Anyway, when I was leaving my room, that's when I noticed it was kinda noisy...

Japanese: そのときはもう、 イサオさんは‥‥？
English: Mr. Corrida was already dead at that time...?

Japanese: 死んでいたみたいです。 マネージャーさんが言ってました。
English: Yeah, that's what I gathered anyway from my manager.

Japanese: （さっきから、何かといえば “マネージャーさん”だな‥‥）
English: (With the way he's always talking about his manager, it's as if she runs his life.)

Japanese: そこに、コートの刑事が 現れたんですよ。
English: And that's when the detective in the green coat showed up.

Japanese: 身体検査されて‥‥いつのまにか、 タイホされちゃったんです。
English: He searched me... And then, out of the blue, the dude arrested me.

Japanese: 亡くなった藤見野　イサオさん とは、どんな‥‥？
English: About you and the victim, Mr. Juan Corrida, what sort of...

Japanese: ベツに、関係ありませんよ。
English: That's got nothing to do with anything, dude.

Japanese: あのカオでボクと同い年なんて、 笑っちゃうよね。
English: Man, with that face of his, you can't even tell he's the same age as me.

Japanese: ムリヤリ《忍者ナンジャ》を 映画化したみたいだけど‥‥
English: And he wanted to try making a "Jammin' Ninja" movie, even though we all know it'd fail.

Japanese: 結果はトノサマンの勝ち、 といったところですね。
English: The Nickel Samurai still won in the end, right?

Japanese: ‥‥そう。 グランプリはいただいたからね。
English: Yeah. I took the Grand Prix by storm.

Japanese: だから、ボクがアイツを 殺す理由なんて、ないんですよ。
English: So why would I, the winner, have any reason to kill the guy anyway?

Japanese: 向こうがこっちをねらうなら ともかく、ね。
English: Dude, you'd think it'd be the other way around, you know?

Japanese: たしかにボク、おカネは イヤになるほどありますけど、
English: Dude, I know I like to throw money around,

Japanese: なんでも買う、ってワケじゃ ないんですよ。
English: but it's not like I buy anything and everything.

Japanese: （売るつもりもないけど）
English: (That's nice, because it's not up for sale anyway!)

Japanese: だから、知りませんよ。 ‥‥記者会見なんて。
English: Dude, like I told you, I don't know anything about that press conference.

Japanese: そういうコトは、 マネージャーさんにまかせてるし。
English: I leave that kind of stuff to my manager.

Japanese: （‥‥どうも引っかかるな‥‥）
English: (...This is harder than pulling bass from a river...)

Japanese: そのトランシーバー、 どうしてベンゴシさんが‥‥？
English: So, why are you carrying around that transceiver...?

Japanese: ぼくが聞きたいですよ。
English: I wanted to ask you about it.

Japanese: それに、どうして 言いなりになってるんですか？
English: And why do you do everything it tells you to do?

Japanese: トランシーバーの指示に‥‥
English: You know, the messages it sends you, dude.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんのコト、コイツには カンケイないからな‥‥）
English: (On second thought, he probably doesn't have anything to do with Maya's kidnapping.)

Japanese: ‥‥ヒミツです。
English: ...That's a secret.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥。
English: OK, whatever, dude.

Japanese: この記事ですけど‥‥。
English: What do you think about this article...?
Hmph.

Japanese: おカタいオンナだと 思ってましたけど‥‥
English: If you're talking about her thing with Juan,

Japanese: あのイサオとねえ‥‥。
English: I always thought she was a bit careless in the way she handled it...

Japanese: それだけですか？
English: And that's it?

Japanese: ‥‥それだけですよ。
English: ...That's it.

Japanese: ‥‥見たことあるカオですね。 ファンクラブのヒトかな。
English: I've seen him somewhere before... Is he a member of my fanclub?

Japanese: 荷星さんです。センパイでしょう？ ‥‥あなたの。
English: That's Mr. Powers. Isn't he supposed to be kind of like a mentor to you?

Japanese: ニボシ‥‥？　ああ、 体操のおニイさん、やってる。
English: Powers...? Oh yeah! He's the guy on that kid's exercise program.

Japanese: このあいだ、 サイン書いてあげましたよ。
English: I gave him an autograph just the other day.

Japanese: （まったく、センパイの立場が ないな‥‥）
English: (He doesn't see Mr. Powers as a mentor at all...)

Japanese: とにかく、ボクは 殺してませんよ。
English: Look, dude, I didn't kill him.

Japanese: トノサマンは、忍者ナンジャに 圧勝したんですからね。
English: I mean, the Nickel Samurai kicked the Jammin' Ninja's butt, right?

Japanese: ボクにしてみれば、 彼などモンダイじゃないし。
English: So it's not like I felt threatened by the likes of him.

Japanese: ‥‥ライバルというのも、 かなしい関係なのですね‥‥
English: Being rivals sounds like a sad way to relate to another person...

Japanese: ボクのマネージャーです。 会ったんですか？
English: That's my manager. Did you meet her?

Japanese: ええ、まあ。
English: Uh, yeah.

Japanese: シッカリしてますよね、彼女。
English: What do you think? Strong woman, right?

Japanese: ボクなんて、いいように あつかわれてますよ‥‥。
English: And she takes good care of me...

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (...You're such a mama's boy...)

Japanese: あの‥‥どうかしましたか？
English: Um... What's wrong?

Japanese: ‥‥どこまで知ってるんだ？
English: How much do you know...?

Japanese: な、なんのことですか‥‥？
English: Wh-What do you mean "how much"...?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士さん。
English: ...Mr. Lawyer.

Japanese: たしかにボクは、あんたに 弁護を依頼しました。
English: I may be your client...

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: But...

Japanese: ヨケイなセンサクは しないでもらえるかな。
English: I hope you will keep yourself out of my personal life.

Japanese: い、いや‥‥ぼくは そんなつもりは‥‥
English: A-Ah, no, I would never...

Japanese: じゃあ、そろそろボクは このへんで。
English: Now, if you'll excuse me...

Japanese: 食事のヤクソクが あるから‥‥。
English: I have a lunch appointment I have to keep...

Japanese: （留置所で、いったいダレと 食事するんだよ！）
English: (You're in DETENTION! Who in the world are you going to eat with!? The security guard!?)

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥？
English: Mr. Nick...?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥天野　由利恵 という女性は‥‥
English: This Celeste Inpax lady...

Japanese: かなり重要なイミを 持った人物らしいね。
English: Somehow, I get the feeling she is a very important person in all this...

Japanese: ハンサムですよね。ホント。ボク。
English: Yeah... I'm such a handsome devil.

Japanese: え、ええ。まあ。
English: Uh... Um, yeah...

Japanese: 空手、柔道、抜刀道、ヤリに シュリケン、小刀護身道に居合道。
English: And I'm way good at sports! Karate, tennis, judo, soccer, football, hockey, fencing...

Japanese: そりゃあ、人気も出ますよね。
English: Dude, it's no wonder I'm so popular.

Japanese: え、ええ。まあ。 （悪気がないぶん、ハラ立つな）
English: Uh... Um, yeah... (You're also good at sitting in a jail cell, Mr. Popular.)

Japanese: そのヒト、ファンクラブの 会員かな。
English: Um, dude, this person looks like a member of my fan club.

Japanese: ちょっと待って。ファンクラブの 会長さんに聞いてみるから。
English: Hold on a sec. I'm gonna ask the fanclub president, OK?

Japanese: いえもう、けっこうです‥‥。
English: Um, "dude", you know what...? Forget I asked...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ホテル・バンドー　ロビー
English: March 21 Gatewater Hotel Hotel Lobby

Japanese: あッ！　コラ！ 勝手に入っちゃ‥‥
English: Hey! What do you think you're doing!? You can't come in without...

Japanese: おはようございます。
English: Good morning.

Japanese: ちょいとアンタッ！ どーいうコトなんだいコレは！
English: Hold on, it's YOU! What is going on around here!?

Japanese: は‥‥はい？
English: U-Uh... Um...

Japanese: イサオちゃん、 死んじゃったんだって？
English: I heard poor Juan was killed. Is that true!?

Japanese: やったの、またトノサマンだった、 って話じゃないか！
English: It's a bad rerun is what this is. Another "Steel Samurai" doing the most evil of deeds.

Japanese: いや、それは‥‥
English: Um, you know... That's not entirely...

Japanese: オバチャンはね！　イサオちゃんの 大ファンだったんだョ！
English: I'll have you know that I was a huge fan of Juan's!

Japanese: どーしてこう、お気に入りのスター ばっかりパタパタ死んでいくのサ！
English: Why, oh why, do all the stars I'm interested in drop one by one like flies!?

Japanese: いっつもそうだョ！　オバチャンが 小学生のときだって、そう！
English: It's always been that way! Ever since I was a little girl in elementary school!

Japanese: クラスで飼ってたハムスター、オバ チャンが当番のときに死んでるのサ
English: The class hamster was fine until it was my turn to clean its cage, and then it died!

Japanese: あだ名は“シニガミ”だったネェも ういいョオバチャン開きなおったョ
English: The other kids started calling me the "Queen Reaper". I swear that's what they dubbed me!

Japanese: アンタにも呪いかけちゃおうかネあ と３分で死ぬイヤむしろすでに死ん
English: "Whoever you name in your evil spell dies within three minutes," they would taunt.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。事件について、 お話を聞きたいんですけど。
English: Um... Actually, I wanted to ask you about the murder and what happened...

Japanese: フン！　勝手におし！
English: Hmph! Don't push me, boy!

Japanese: あの。なるほどくん。
English: Um... Mr. Nick?

Japanese: わたくし、ちょっと聞きとりにくい のですけど。‥‥早くて。
English: I... Um... I couldn't hear everything she said... because she was talking too fast...

Japanese: オバチャン。 ゆっくりおねがいします。
English: Ms. Oldbag, could you please speak a little slower...?

Japanese: ヨケイなお世話だョ！
English: Don't boss me around, you spiky-haired, smarty-pants!

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: 取材に来た人たちの カメラだろう。
English: These cameras must belong to the press that came to cover the conference.

Japanese: どれも本格的なシロモノで、 ステージをねらっている。
English: They're all basically doing the same thing: staring at the empty stage.

Japanese: 吹き抜けのロビーは、 とにかく大きな空間に感じる。
English: A lobby with a grand staircase always feels enormous.

Japanese: そして今、この空間には、 事件の緊張感がみなぎっている。
English: But right now, the only thing filling this enormous room is anxiety about the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥これ以上、悪いことが 起こらなければいいけど‥‥。
English: ...I really hope nothing else bad happens here...

Japanese: アトラクション・ショーの ステージだ。
English: It's the stage they were going to use for the post-ceremony stage show.

Japanese: ショーのあと、記者会見も 予定されていたというけど‥‥
English: After the show, there was supposed to be a press conference, but...

Japanese: ‥‥オートロさん、 ご存じないみたいでしたね。
English: ...Even Mr. Engarde himself didn't seem to know, huh?

Japanese: （本人が知らない、なんて コト、あるのかなあ‥‥）
English: (How does the person who's supposed to give a conference not know about it...?)

Japanese: アトラクション・ショーと 記者会見のために用意されたイス。
English: Seats for the spectators of the post-ceremony show and press conference.

Japanese: ‥‥両方とも、 ムダになってしまった。
English: But it's too bad neither event is going to be held now.

Japanese: １年前の事件で、大好きだった イブクロちゃんが死んじゃってサ。
English: My dear Hammer died a year ago in that dreadful murder.

Japanese: やっと最近、またムネがキュン、 ってなるスター、めっけたのに。
English: And only recently did I finally find a star that makes this heart go *badump* again.

Japanese: ‥‥おキノドクです。
English: ...I don't know what to say...

Japanese: なんで死ぬかネ。オバチャンが ホレたスターばっかり。
English: I ask you, why does every star I cheer for always ends up kicking the bucket?

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: イサオちゃんをワルく言うヤツァ、 ユルさないんだからネ！
English: I'd watch your words! No one's going to get away with saying anything bad about my Juan!

Japanese: 言いませんよ‥‥。
English: But I haven't said anything...

Japanese: オバチャン、信じないョ！ あんなオンナの言うコトなんか！
English: Well, I don't believe a word that woman says anyway!

Japanese: あんなオンナ‥‥？
English: Huh? What woman?

Japanese: 下品な大阪弁でくっちゃべる、 小うるさいコのコト。
English: That irritating, backwater girl with the afro and the horrible country accent.

Japanese: だいたい、なんだいアレは。 べらべら、オシャベリばかり。
English: I mean, what is that manner of speaking supposed to be? And why does she never stop?

Japanese: ちったぁ“つつしみ”ってコトバを 知らないのかね最近のムスメッコは
English: Honestly, women these days. They don't know the meaning of the word "modesty".

Japanese: アレョオバチャンが若いコロはそう まさに花も恥じらうオトメってヤツ
English: When I was a young maiden, I was so beautiful even the flowers of the field blushed.

Japanese: これでもサ押し花とかウラナイとか コックリさんとか詩を書いたりラブ
English: But despite this, I pressed flowers, or read the stars, or wrote beautiful love poems or

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん。ノドかわかない？
English: ...Pearls? Are you thirsty?

Japanese: そうですね。すこし。
English: Um, a little.

Japanese: じゃ、ジュースでも 買ってきてあげるよ。
English: OK. I'll go get you some juice or something.

Japanese: あ。ありがとうございます。
English: Th-Thank you very much.

Japanese: ‥‥ってちょっと！ ヒトの話を聞きなさいョ！
English: Hey! Are you paying attention!? Young'uns today!

Japanese: （オバチャン‥‥ナツミさんから 何か聞いたのかな‥‥？）
English: (So I'm guessing Ms. Oldbag heard everything from Lotta...)

Japanese: 事件があったときのこと ですけど‥‥
English: I want to ask you about what happened around the time of the murder...

Japanese: ああ。オバチャン知らないョ。 ずっとここで、準備してたから。
English: Ah, I don't know anything about that. I was here getting ready.

Japanese: ジュンビ‥‥？
English: Getting ready? For what...?

Japanese: トノサマンのショー。 ‥‥あと、記者会見ね。
English: The show, of course! ...Well, and the press conference afterwards.

Japanese: （例の、ナゾの記者会見‥‥か）
English: (Cue mysterious music for the magical press conference.)

Japanese: とにかく、事件のコトは 知らないんだけどネ。
English: Anyway, I don't know anything about the murder.

Japanese: そうですか‥‥
English: Ah, I see...

Japanese: ‥‥ただ。
English: But.

Japanese: 例によって、見るべきモノ だけは、キチンと見ちゃったョ！
English: But if you're talking about what I saw, that's different. I saw it very clearly!

Japanese: えッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: 目撃しちゃったのョ、オバチャン。 決定的な瞬間を、サ！
English: I saw the most important moment of the night!

Japanese: あの‥‥“決定的な瞬間”って！
English: Th-The most important moment!? You don't mean...!?

Japanese: お。目の色が変わったネ。
English: Oh, so now you treat me with respect, you disrespectful child.

Japanese: それそれ。目上のヒトと話すときは そういう、尊敬のマナザシでネ。
English: When you speak to your elders, you should always be polite. Really, kids today.

Japanese: 聞かせてください！ ナニを見たんですか！
English: Please tell me! What did you see!?

Japanese: この前の事件じゃァ、 ヒドい目にあったからね。
English: The murder last night was gruesome, wasn't it? But then, what murder isn't?

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (Please don't stray onto another tangent... Please...)

Japanese: 聞きたかったら、手みやげの ひとつも持ってくるんだネ！
English: If you want to hear more, then show your respect and bring this lady a present!

Japanese: 教えてください！　事件の夜、 何を見たんですか。
English: Please tell me! What did you see on the night of the murder!?

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: お‥‥おねがいします！ 真宵さまのイノチが‥‥
English: P-Please! Mystic Maya's life is...

Japanese: タダじゃあ、教えられないねェ。
English: Ah, but you haven't given me my present yet.

Japanese: ‥‥そおだねえ‥‥
English: ...Hmm... Let's see...

Japanese: たとえばホレ、イサオちゃんの サインとかほしいネ、オバチャン！
English: I'll only tell it to you if you can bring me Juan's autograph!

Japanese: （好きなコト言ってるぞ‥‥）
English: (What an unreasonable request... You might as well have asked for the moon!)

Japanese: アンタ、キノドクだったねェ。
English: I feel bad for you now.
Huh...?

Japanese: オバチャン、あの夜、 見ちまったんだ。
English: I tell you, I saw him that night.

Japanese: アイツが、イサオちゃんの 控え室から出てくるトコ。
English: I saw him coming out of Juan's room.

Japanese: なんですって！
English: You're kidding!

Japanese: そんな！
English: Oh no!

Japanese: 死体が発見される、 １０分ぐらい前かネ。
English: It was about ten minutes before Juan's body was discovered.

Japanese: ちょっとトイレに行ったとき、 グーゼン、ネ。
English: It was just a coincidence. I was on my way to the toilet, minding my own business.

Japanese: そ、その話‥‥警察には？
English: And...? Did you tell that to the police?

Japanese: モチロン、タレこんでやったョ。 図書券でも、もらえるかと思って。
English: Well, of course! I thought I could get a gift certificate or two out of it, maybe more.

Japanese: としょけん‥‥
English: Gift certificate...?

Japanese: またスカウトされちゃったワケ。 ‥‥明日の法廷。
English: I've been recruited again for that part at the trial. You know, the trial tomorrow?

Japanese: 今度こそ有罪にしてやるからねェ！ アンタの依頼人。
English: This time, you're gonna get it! I'm gonna work hard to get your client pronounced guilty!

Japanese: オートロさんに、 何かウラミでも‥‥
English: But Mr. Engarde hasn't done anything bad...

Japanese: ウラミもナニも、アイツが‥‥ オートロがやったんだよ！
English: I don't care about details like that! I know he did my dear poor Juan in! I just do!

Japanese: あの、ヒキョウなオトコが！
English: That yellow-bellied chicken!

Japanese: ひきょう‥‥？
English: A yellow-bellied chicken...? I wonder what that would look like...

Japanese: オバチャン、知ってるんだョ！ オートロがナニをやったか！
English: I trust my senses. I know when someone did something bad, and I say he did it!

Japanese: （オートロさんが、いったい ‥‥ナニをしたって言うんだ？）
English: (What did Mr. Engarde ever do to her to deserve this...?)

Japanese: いったい、オートロさんが 何をしたと‥‥？
English: What did Mr. Engarde do to you to make you so...

Japanese: そんなコトも知らないのかい？
English: You don't know!?

Japanese: ヤツはね！　デッチ上げようと したんだよ！
English: That guy! He framed my Juan!

Japanese: イサオちゃんの スキャンダルをね！
English: He created that scandal that plagued poor Juan!

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: な、なに？
English: Wh-What is it?

Japanese: なんですか？ ‥‥“すきゃんだる”って。
English: What's a "skan-dal"?

Japanese: あ、ああ。 ‥‥帰ってから教えてあげるから。
English: Oh... Um, I'll tell you about that after we get home, OK?

Japanese: かわいそうに、イサオちゃんは オンナの色香に迷わされて‥‥
English: Poor Juan, led astray by the wiles of that vile temptress...

Japanese: なるほどくん。 “おんなのいろか”というのは‥‥
English: Mr. Nick... What do "viles" and "wile temptress" mean...?

Japanese: ‥‥ぐッ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥とにかく今は、 オバチャンのハナシを聞こう！
English: Um, how about we just listen to what Ms. Oldbag has to say for now, OK Pearls?

Japanese: オバチャン、その “オンナ”って、いったい‥‥？
English: So, Ms. Oldbag. Who is this "woman" you're talking about...?

Japanese: ‥‥華宮　霧緒だよ！
English: ...Adrian Andrews, of course! Who else!?

Japanese: オートロのヤツ、イサオちゃんに コイツをさし向けたんだ！
English: That guy, he shoved the girl onto Juan on purpose!

Japanese: 自分のマネージャーを、ですか？ なんのために‥‥
English: His own manager? But why...?

Japanese: 決まってるだろ！　スキャンダルで イサオちゃんを失脚させるためョ！
English: I thought lawyers were smart! It was to create a scandal to make Juan lose face!

Japanese: ‥‥イサオちゃん、そのオンナに ホネヌキにされちまったんだ！
English: That girl drove Juan into a scandal that dragged his reputation through the mud!

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、テキトーな スキャンダルだなあ‥‥）
English: (Sounds like a pretty standard definition of a scandal to me...)

Japanese: どうしてそんなハナシ、 オバチャンが知ってるんですか？
English: Why do you know about that anyway, Ms. Oldbag?

Japanese: オバチャン、イサオちゃんの 大ファンなんだョ！
English: I'm one of Juan's biggest fans!

Japanese: 情報を集めるためなら、 なんだってしちゃうっての！
English: I'm always out there gathering information! There's nothing I don't know!

Japanese: オートロさんがそんなことをした っていう証拠は‥‥？
English: And do you have proof that Mr. Engarde did what you say he did...?

Japanese: 来週発売される雑誌を 見るんだネ！
English: Next week's issue of a certain magazine says so!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ やっぱり、ゴシップ記事か‥‥）
English: (Ugh... Of course. A tabloid...)

Japanese: 来週‥‥ということは、まだ 世間には知られてないのですね。
English: Next week... Doesn't that mean it's something people don't know about yet...?

Japanese: （そんな情報を、 なぜオバチャンが‥‥？）
English: (Why would Ms. Oldbag have information like that...? And where did she get it...?)

Japanese: ヒッヒッヒッ‥‥
English: Hee hee hee...

Japanese: アンタにやる情報なんて ないョ！
English: I don't have anything to say to delinquents like you!

Japanese: （うう‥‥自分のカラに 引きこもってしまったぞ‥‥）
English: (Grrr... She's clamming up like the old clam she is...)

Japanese: おねがいします！ なんでもいいですから！
English: Please! Anything would be helpful!

Japanese: じゃ、オバチャンのスリーサイズ 教えてやろうか？
English: Well then, how about I tell you my measurements?

Japanese: ‥‥それはいいです。
English: ...Um... No, that's okay... Really.

Japanese: なるほどくん、人気が ないのですね‥‥
English: She really doesn't like you, does she Mr. Nick?

Japanese: メンボクない。
English: I know, I know...

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- What You Witnessed --

Japanese: ショージキなところ、ベツに しゃべったってイイんだけどサ。
English: Alright, I'll be honest with you for now.

Japanese: じゃあ、教えてくださいよ！
English: Then, please! Tell us what you saw!

Japanese: もったいないじゃないのサ。
English: But, aww, what a waste.

Japanese: 若い男のコをホンロウできる チャンスなのに。
English: And here I have a perfectly good chance to have a little fun at you young'un's expense.

Japanese: だってホレ、オバチャン、 魔性の女だからサ。
English: Because I am a little devil, after all.

Japanese: （魔性というか悪性というか‥‥）
English: (Um... Doesn't that imply you aren't a good person...?)

Japanese: しかたない。オバチャンが ほしがりそうなものは‥‥
English: Alright, I'll give you what you want...

Japanese: アンタ、アレだろ。 オートロのベンゴシなんだろ？
English: You're Engarde's lawyer, aren't you?

Japanese: え、ええ。そうですけど。
English: W-Well, yes... but...

Japanese: ゼーッタイ、 教えないもんネ！
English: Then forget it! There is no way I'm telling you!

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか！
English: B-But why!?

Japanese: そ‥‥それ！ イサオちゃんのサイン！
English: Th-Th-That's! That's Juan's autograph!!

Japanese: そうです。
English: Yes, it is.

Japanese: し、しかも！ “カオルちゃんへ”だって！
English: And... And it even says, "To my dearest Wendy" on it!

Japanese: それ、オバチャンのコトだョ！
English: Th-That's me, right!? Right!?
Um...

Japanese: “大場　カオル”だからね！ オバチャン。
English: My name is Wendy Oldbag, so that "Wendy" has to be me, right!?

Japanese: （たしかに“カオル”なの かもしれない‥‥）
English: (Well, it may say "Wendy"...)

Japanese: （でも、決して“カオルちゃん” ではないと思う）
English: (But somehow, I don't think Juan had THIS "Wendy" in mind when he signed it...)

Japanese: ねえ、おくれよぉ！ おくれったらあ！
English: Oh, please, give it to me! Let me have it! Please!

Japanese: 当然‥‥タダで、というワケには いきませんねえ。
English: Uh, un. I can't let you have it, just like that.

Japanese: わかってるわョ！ これでどーだいッ！
English: Yes, yes, I know. Then how about an exchange!?

Japanese: （パリンパリンとまあ、 気前のいいコト‥‥）
English: (Wow, she must really want this autograph...)

Japanese: ‥‥まだダメかいッ！ よおおおし‥‥
English: My offer isn't good enough for you!? Fine, Mr. Wright. You win.

Japanese: オバチャン、 もお‥‥見せちゃうゥッ！
English: Wendy Oldbag, ready to open up her heart! All for my dearest Juan!

Japanese: 《サイン色紙》を カオルちゃんにあげた。
English: Autograph given to Wendy dearest.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ホテル・バンドー　すみれの間
English: March 21 Gatewater Hotel Viola Hall

Japanese: 事情聴取、まだ つづいているのだろうか。
English: Looks like the investigation is still in full swing.

Japanese: 警官やホテルのスタッフが いそがしそうに動いている。
English: The hotel staff and the police are running around like a bunch of headless chickens.

Japanese: こんな状況で、調査が できるのでしょうか‥‥
English: I wonder if we can do any investigating of our own in this kind of atmosphere...

Japanese: ‥‥やるしかないね。
English: ...Well, got to roll up the sleeves and try, I guess.

Japanese: ‥‥ゆうべ、お食事した テーブルですね。
English: We ate at this table last night, didn't we?

Japanese: そうだね。
English: Yes.

Japanese: あのときは、本当に わたくし、しあわせでした。
English: I was really happy then.

Japanese: （もう一度、エガオを 取りもどさないとな‥‥）
English: (I wish I could make you smile again... Even for a second...)

Japanese: 料理はまだ かたづけられていない。
English: They haven't cleaned up all the food yet.

Japanese: パーティーは、終わってしまうと とてもサビシイ気がするんだ。
English: There's a sad feeling hanging in the air now that the party is over...

Japanese: ゆうべ、授賞式が行われた ステージだ。
English: The awards ceremony was held on that stage just last night.

Japanese: ‥‥ゴージャスだったな、 さすがに。
English: It was really fabulous.

Japanese: ちょっと、あのサーカスを 思い出しました。
English: You just reminded me of the circus for a second.

Japanese: みなさん、おゲンキでしょうか？
English: I wonder if everyone is alright.

Japanese: （タチミ・サーカスは最近、 公演を再開したらしい）
English: (I heard that Berry Big Circus just recently started holding performances again...)

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、ゲンキに やってると思うよ。
English: I'm sure they're all fine, Pearls.

Japanese: それにしても、 ゴウカなホテルだ。
English: This sure is one luxurious hotel.

Japanese: 何かにとりつかれたかのように、 ゴテゴテかざりつけされている。
English: Almost to the point of gaudy, with how it blends together everything "fancy" imaginable.

Japanese: （そういえば去年、あのボーイさん から暑中見舞いがきてたっけ）
English: (Speaking of fancy, didn't that bellboy give me something like that last year?)

Japanese: 大げさなロビーに通じる、 大げさなドアが見える。
English: There's a grand set of doors over there.

Japanese: ボーイにつれられた真宵ちゃんが 消えたドアだ。
English: It's the doors Maya followed the bellboy out of, only to disappear...

Japanese: ‥‥あのとき、ぼくたちが ついていれば‥‥
English: ...If only we had all gone together...

Japanese: わたくし、このような キレイなもの、初めて見ました。
English: That is such a beautiful sight.

Japanese: シャンデリア？
English: The chandelier?

Japanese: はい。‥‥こんな美しい 明かりの下で、
English: Yes. But I can't believe it...

Japanese: このようなヒドい事件が 起こるなんて‥‥
English: I can't believe that such a terrible murder happened under such beautiful lights...

Japanese: ショックです。
English: It's shocking.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ホテル・バンドー　すみれの間
English: March 21 Gatewater Hotel Viola Hall

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　どうも。
English: Oh, Mr. Wright! How are you?

Japanese: あ。荷星さん。 まだ帰れないんですか？
English: Ah, Mr. Powers. Have you been here the entire time?

Japanese: ええ。関係者は、ホテルから 出してもらえないんですよ。
English: Yeah. People connected to the murder aren't allowed to go home, let alone leave...

Japanese: 《トノサマン・丙！》のこと、 教えてもらえますか？
English: Can you tell me a little more about the Nickel Samurai TV show?

Japanese: いいですよ。
English: OK.

Japanese: 《トノサマン・丙！》は、 お子さま向けのヒーロー番組で‥‥
English: "The Nickel Samurai" is an action hero program aimed towards kids.

Japanese: 《大江戸戦士トノサマン》の 続編にあたります。
English: It's the sequel to "The Steel Samurai".

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: 今度のトノサマンは３兄弟。 甲・乙・丙となっておりまして、
English: This time, there are three Samurai Brothers: Aluminum Samurai, Tin Samurai...

Japanese: 江戸の町を舞台に、 恋のさやあてをします。
English: And of course, the Nickel Samurai. It's a love "Y" in Neo Olde Tokyo!

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。 ‥‥って、なんですって？
English: I see... W-Wait...

Japanese: ナニのナニあて‥‥？
English: A love what...?

Japanese: 恋のさやあて。
English: A love "Y".

Japanese: 茶屋のサヨちゃんに、 ３兄弟は同時に恋を。
English: This girl, Sayo, works at a tea shop and all three guys fall for her at the same time.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥
English: Oh... I guess the "Y" shape is like the 3 brothers colliding over this one girl?

Japanese: しかしサヨちゃんは、悪の組織 エチゴグループのムスメだった！
English: Anyway, Sayo is actually the daughter of the evil Strawberry Clan's leader!

Japanese: ‥‥とんでもない設定ですね。
English: Sounds like an unusual situation. Like Romeo and Juliet... times three.

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: Y-Yeah...

Japanese: しかし、このせつない恋物語が ＯＬに大ウケでして‥‥。
English: Strange thing is, this sort of forbidden love story is really big with the office ladies.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。
English: Um...

Japanese: はい？
English: Yes, Pearl?

Japanese: その先はッ！ その先は、どうなるのでしょうか？
English: Wh-What happens next!? I want to know!

Japanese: サヨさまは‥‥サヨさまは、 恋に生きるのですよね？
English: Ms. Sayo... Does Ms. Sayo fall in love? She does, doesn't she?

Japanese: ‥‥毎週日曜日、朝８時です。
English: Every Sunday at 8 AM.

Japanese: 今週は『世界こども名作劇場』は お休みしましょう。
English: I'm going to stop watching "Kids' Masterpiece Theatre" starting this week!

Japanese: （ホンキだ‥‥！）
English: (I can't believe she's really considering it!)

Japanese: 《忍者ナンジャ》って、 どんな番組なんですか‥‥？
English: So what's the "Jammin' Ninja" TV show like?

Japanese: もともとは、映画のリメイクから 始まったんですけどね。
English: It started from a remake of an old movie, to tell you the truth.

Japanese: こちらも、お子さま向け ヒーロー番組です。
English: The Jammin' Ninja is, like the Samurai shows, aimed towards kids.

Japanese: うだつの上がらない忍者が、 スターダムにのし上がる物語。
English: It's the story of a ninja who can't climb a wall, but became a big pop star.
...Uh, what?

Japanese: 忍者としてはダメなナンジャ。 でも彼には、すばらしい武器が。
English: He was a really lousy ninja. Absolutely couldn't do any ninja things right at all.

Japanese: それは、ウツクシイ声！
English: But boy could he sing!

Japanese: 彼はまっかなギターを片手に、 室町時代の芸能界に殴りこみます。
English: With his trusty bright red guitar in hand, he took the ancient world by storm.

Japanese: まま、まっかなギター？ 忍者が？
English: A... A ninja... With a bright red guitar...!?

Japanese: そして、イトしいミソラ姫の前で ついに最後の決戦が！
English: And then, the final fight in front of his beloved Princess Misola!

Japanese: “室町フォーク５番勝負” ‥‥その第３戦、前夜！
English: "Jammin' versus the Muromachi Five!"

Japanese: なんと！　‥‥ナンジャは カゼをひいてしまったのです！
English: Suddenly! Our brave hero catches a not so Jammin' cold the night before Battle 3!

Japanese: ‥‥ヒドい話ですね。
English: Aww, that's too bad for him...

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: Y-Yeah...

Japanese: しかし、このポップな恋物語が 女子高生に大ウケでして‥‥。
English: But this kind of pop music- based love story is something high school girls really like.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。
English: Um...

Japanese: はい？
English: Yes, Pearl?

Japanese: その先はッ！ その先は、どうなるのでしょうか？
English: Wh-What happens next!? I want to know!

Japanese: ナンジャは‥‥ナンジャは、 歌えるのですか！　ミソラ姫はッ！
English: Jammin'... The Jammin' Ninja! Will he be able to sing!? What about Princess Misola!?

Japanese: ‥‥毎週日曜日、朝８時です。
English: Every Sunday at 8 AM.

Japanese: うううう‥‥わたくしはいったい、 何を見れば‥‥
English: Umm... Which show should I watch...? Hmm...

Japanese: （ホンキだ‥‥！）
English: (I can't believe she's really considering it!)

Japanese: 聞かせてください。 “ウワサ”って、なんですか？
English: Would you mind telling me about this "gossip"?

Japanese: いやぁ、お好きですねえ 成歩堂さんも、ヤッパリ。
English: Ah, so you're interested in it too! I figured you would be!

Japanese: え。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: ボクも目がないんですよ。 芸能界のウワサ話。
English: I have such a weakness for celebrity gossip too.

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか。
English: O-Oh really... You too, huh...?

Japanese: いやね、ホラ。 コレなんですけど。
English: Yeah. So take a look at this!

Japanese: （オバチャンが読みそうな ウサンくさい芸能雑誌だ）
English: (Looks like a tabloid Ms. Oldbag would read...)

Japanese: なになに。 “忍者ナンジャ、深夜の密会！”
English: Alright, let's see here... "Jammin' Midnight Rendezvous!?"

Japanese: “お相手は、マネージャーの Ｋ．Ｋか？”
English: "...to the mysterious, yet beautiful manager to the stars, Ms. A. A.!"

Japanese: ‥‥ね。おわかりでしょ？
English: ...Y-You see now, don't you?

Japanese: 何がですか‥‥？
English: What...?

Japanese: もお！　トボけちゃ イケませんよ成歩堂さん！
English: You can stop pretending to be in the dark, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: イサオくんには、自分の マネージャーはいないんですよ。
English: Juan Corrida didn't have a manager of his own.

Japanese: それなら、マネージャーの Ｋ．Ｋさんといえば！
English: Which means if we're talking about a certain manager with the initials A. A....

Japanese: ‥‥華宮　霧緒‥‥？
English: Adrian Andrews...?

Japanese: そ。そうなんですよ！ コレはニュースです！
English: Y-Yes, exactly! This is big news!!

Japanese: （でも、なんかおかしいな。 あの霧緒さんが‥‥）
English: (But... It seems kind of odd. That woman, Ms. Andrews...)

Japanese: （よりによって、イチバンの ライバルと‥‥）
English: (Together with the biggest rival of her client...?)

Japanese: それはホラ。オトコとオンナの コトですから。
English: Ah, it's that wonderful thing that can only happen between two people...

Japanese: うれしそうですね。 ‥‥荷星さん。
English: Mr. Powers looks so happy.

Japanese: （春美ちゃんも、ワケも わからず笑っている‥‥）
English: (Pearls is just following along, not having any idea as to why he's smiling...)

Japanese: ‥‥ま。たしかに、 なんの証拠もありませんけどね。
English: Well, like the saying goes, one man's garbage is another man's treasure.

Japanese: 《雑誌の記事》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Magazine Clipping added to the Court Record.

Japanese: いやー、そいつには お世話になりました。
English: Ah, I really owe you one.

Japanese: 成歩堂さんたちがいなかったら ボクはどうなっていたことか‥‥
English: If it wasn't for you guys, I don't know what would've happened.

Japanese: 本当に、大変なおシゴトだと 思いますよ。
English: What you did... It was a real tight spot I was in, and you fought so hard to get me out.

Japanese: ボク、何があっても 一生忘れませんから。
English: I don't care what happens, I'll never forget everything you've done for me.

Japanese: ヘンですよね。 いきなり決まったんですよ。
English: They called this so suddenly that something about it just seems off, you know?

Japanese: 記者会見、ですか？
English: You mean the press conference?

Japanese: ええ。“もし受賞したら コクハクしたいことがある”って。
English: Yeah. He said, "There's something I'd like to confess if I win."

Japanese: ファックスが入ったそうですよ。 ‥‥きのう。
English: I heard he faxed his request for a conference in to the office only yesterday.

Japanese: きのう‥‥。 （たしかに、いきなりだな）
English: Yesterday... (Yeah, that is kind of sudden.)

Japanese: オートロくんのマネージャーが 手配したらしいですけどね。
English: But I guess his manager set everything up somehow, so he was lucky.

Japanese: へえ‥‥。
English: Hmm... Nice manager.

Japanese: ‥‥まさか、ユーカイ犯 だったとは‥‥
English: I can't believe it... A kidnapping...

Japanese: どんなヒトだったか、 おぼえていませんか？
English: Do you remember what the person looked like who gave this to you?

Japanese: あのボーイさんですよね。 それがそのお‥‥
English: It was that bellboy. And...

Japanese: フツウのおジイさん でしたからねえ‥‥。
English: And I didn't suspect a thing because he looked like such a normal old man...

Japanese: （おジイさん‥‥か‥‥）
English: (Old man, huh...)

Japanese: あ、そのギターケース。 イサオくんの‥‥
English: That guitar case... That was Juan's...

Japanese: よくご存じですね。
English: I'm guessing you've seen this case before?

Japanese: それはもう。有名ですから。 ‥‥まっかなギター。
English: It's pretty famous by now. It stands out because it holds THE bright red guitar.

Japanese: （忍者ナンジャの トレードマークだもんな‥‥）
English: (The bright red guitar... The Jammin' Ninja's signature item.)

Japanese: そんなダイジなギター‥‥ 授賞式で忘れるでしょうか？
English: But if the guitar is so important, how could he forget it for the award ceremony?

Japanese: （ちょっと引っかかるよな‥‥）
English: (That does seem a little strange...)

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: S-Sorry.

Japanese: せっかく、そのようなものを つきつけていただいても、
English: Thanks for taking the time to take it out and show it to me,

Japanese: モウシワケありませんが、 心あたりがございません。
English: but I'm really sorry, I don't know a thing about it.

Japanese: あの。真宵さんの 妹さんですか？
English: So are you Maya's little sister?

Japanese: いえ。ちがいます。
English: I'm sorry, but no.

Japanese: あ。じゃあ、成歩堂さんの 妹さん。
English: Oh, then you must be Mr. Wright's little sister!

Japanese: そうではありませんけど。
English: Again, I'm sorry, but...

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、じゃあ どなたの妹さんなんですか？
English: Then... Whose little sister are you?

Japanese: すみません。 ‥‥ひとりっ子なんです。
English: I'm sorry, but I'm an only child.

Japanese: あ、そうなんですか。 ‥‥きょ、恐縮です。
English: Oh, I see... S-Sorry.

Japanese: （おたがいに恐縮しあっている）
English: (Pearl looks so sad... She looks really sorry too...)

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、ブジ 帰ってきますよ。
English: I... I'm sure he won't hurt her and he'll let her go soon...

Japanese: ええ‥‥。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。 あなたならだいじょうぶです！
English: Mr. Wright. I believe in you. You will save her!

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thanks.

Japanese: そんなにコワいんですかねえ、 このカオ。
English: Hey, is my face really all that scary?

Japanese: 子どもたちはだれも 近よって来ないし‥‥
English: Kids won't come anywhere near me...

Japanese: ボクがアタマをかくと、 みなさんビクッとするし‥‥
English: But when I cover my face, they're all shocked because then they figure out who I am.

Japanese: わたくしは、とても やさしいおカオだと思います。
English: I think your face makes you look like a very kind person!

Japanese: （荷星さん、 ナミダぐんじゃったぞ‥‥）
English: (Aww... Mr. Powers is getting teary eyed.)

Japanese: ‥‥まさか、こんなところで また会うなんて。
English: I never would've thought I'd see her here of all places.

Japanese: この前の事件では、ヒドい目に あいましたからねえ。
English: The last time we saw each other was during that really bad incident... You know...

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、今ではもう いい思い出ですけど。
English: I mean, when I look back now, I think of the good things, so it's not so bad, I guess.

Japanese: その思い出を、もう一度 味わってるんですよ‥‥ぼく。
English: Yeah, I would love to go back in time and relive a few of those good things... *chuckle*

Japanese: それは‥‥笑いゴトじゃ ないですねえ。
English: Th-That's not funny...

Japanese: （ホントに‥‥）
English: (Yeah, I guess not...)

Japanese: デビューが近かったんですよ。 オートロくんと。
English: He debuted around the same time as Matt in everything, you know.

Japanese: そうなのですか。
English: Really?

Japanese: “スター・のどじまん大会”やら “スター・水泳大会”やら‥‥
English: It started out small... First it was singing contests, then swimming competitions...

Japanese: “スター・ボウリング大会”やら “スター・大忘年会”やら‥‥
English: Then it was bowling tournaments, and then it was who could throw the best

Japanese: ナニかと張り合っていましたねえ。
English: New Year's parties... Juan was always trying to one-up Matt.

Japanese: 最近は、だんだん エスカレートしてきて‥‥
English: But lately, those two were escalating to more and more dangerous things...

Japanese: タイヘンなコトになるんじゃないか って、シンパイしてたんですよ。
English: I thought that no good would come of it all, so I began to worry.

Japanese: （そしてついに‥‥殺人事件が 起こってしまったワケか）
English: (Too bad Juan's story ended so soon...)

Japanese: オートロくん、ボクの 後輩なんですけどね。
English: Matt's younger than me,

Japanese: やっぱり、出てますよ。 スターのオーラが。
English: but you can practically see his star potential.

Japanese: すたーのおおら‥‥ですか。
English: His star po-ten-shial...?

Japanese: サインもらっちゃいましたもん、 ボク。
English: I got his autograph the other day.

Japanese: ‥‥それはちょっと、 カッコわるいですね。
English: ...Feels kind of wrong now, doesn't it?

Japanese: オートロくんがイサオくんを‥‥ そんなコト、ボクは信じません！
English: I don't care what people say! Matt didn't kill Juan! I know he didn't!

Japanese: 霧緒さんですね！ オートロくんのマネージャー。
English: Hey, that's Ms. Andrews! She's Matt's manager.

Japanese: ‥‥じつは、ちょっとだけ アコガレてたりするんですよ‥‥
English: Actually, I was interested in her for a little bit. Just a little...

Japanese: （こういうタイプが スキなのか‥‥荷星さん）
English: (Hmm... So Mr. Powers likes this type of woman...)

Japanese: 何かご存じないですか？ ‥‥霧緒さんのこと。
English: What do you know about Ms. Andrews...?

Japanese: そうですね。その。 ご存じないワケではないような‥‥
English: Well, see... Here's the thing. I don't really KNOW her know her... you know?

Japanese: ‥‥彼女、今ちょっと、 ウワサになってるんですよ。
English: There's sort of a small rumor going around about her right now...

Japanese: ウワサ‥‥ですか？
English: A rumor...?

Japanese: あ！　なんでしたら、 詳しくお話ししましょうか！
English: Ah! If you're interested, I can give you a little bit of the details!

Japanese: （なんか、ウレシそうだな‥‥）
English: (He's so happy, he looks like a lion that's just found his next meal...)

Japanese: ううう‥‥どうも そのようなかたは。
English: Umm... I'm honored you're asking me,

Japanese: 恐縮ですが。その。 ‥‥思い出せなくて。ええ。
English: But I have no idea who this person is. Umm... Sorry...

Japanese: そ、そこまで恐縮しなくても。
English: Ah, it's OK. You don't have to apologize so much.

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: S-Sorry.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 控え室前　ろうか
English: March 21 Gatewater Hotel Hallway

Japanese: あ！　来よった！
English: Hey! Yer here!

Japanese: いやあ。待ってたんやで、 ナルホドー。
English: Been waitin' for ya, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: ナツミさん‥‥
English: Lotta...

Japanese: おまわりさあん！ ハンニンが現れたでェ！
English: Hey! Mr. Cop fella! The thief's showed his face!

Japanese: なな、なんだなんだ。
English: Wh-Wh-What!?

Japanese: タイホやッ！　サイバンやッ！ ユーザイやッ！　シケイやッ！
English: Arrest him! Put him on trial! Find him guilty! Give him the death penalty!

Japanese: ど、どうしたんですか？ ナツミさん。
English: Wh-What's wrong, Lotta!? Are you feeling alright?

Japanese: どうしたもこうしたも ナツミさんもあるかい！
English: Looked here and there and up and down the mountain, but it ain't here!

Japanese: ‥‥ホレホレ、早よ出さんかッ！
English: So why don't ya just hurry up and give it back to me, ya creep!

Japanese: あの、何を‥‥？
English: Um, what are you looking for...?

Japanese: カメラやカメラ！
English: My CAMERA! C-A-M-E-R-A!

Japanese: ほーどーじんのイノチ、 ７万円もしたカメラやぁッ！
English: It's my life blood! I'm gonna die without my $700 camera!!

Japanese: カメラ‥‥ですか？
English: Y-Your camera...?

Japanese: ホレ、よく言うやん。ハンニンは 現場に戻ってくる、って。
English: Look, don't lots of people say the criminal always goes back to the scene of the crime!?

Japanese: ミゴト、引っかかりおったワ！
English: And lookie lookie, here ya are!

Japanese: ミゴト、引っかかったみたいだよ。 ‥‥ヤッカイなトラブルに。
English: Yep. Here I am... Faced with a Lotta trouble.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Huh!?

Japanese: 楽屋の前に花があるのは ふつうだけど、
English: I can understand flowers in front of the dressing rooms,

Japanese: なんでこんなところに クマのぬいぐるみが‥‥？
English: but what are stuffed bears doing here...?

Japanese: とってへん！　とってへんでえ！ ウチやない！　ゼッタイやッ！
English: I didn't touch them! Wasn't me, I tell ya! I give ya my word!

Japanese: （どうやらナツミさんが １ケ、シッケイしたらしい）
English: (Why do I get the impression Lotta helped herself to one of them...?)

Japanese: “王都楼さん江 英都プロダクション一同”‥‥
English: "To Mr. Engarde From the Global Studio Staff"

Japanese: 弁護士にも花輪が あればいいのにな。
English: Ah, it'd be nice if lawyers got flowers too. Something like,

Japanese: “成歩堂さん江 依頼人一同”‥‥とか。
English: "To Mr. Wright From All Your Grateful Clients".

Japanese: ドアに張り紙がある。 “王都楼　真悟様　控え室”
English: There's a piece of paper taped to the door that says, "Matt Engarde's Room".

Japanese: 休憩時間、ここにいたんですよね ‥‥オートロさん。
English: So Mr. Engarde was here during his break, right?

Japanese: そう言ってたね。
English: That's what he claims.

Japanese: （事件の現場‥‥イサオさんの 控え室に行くには‥‥）
English: (Hmm... The crime scene... If he had wanted to get to Mr. Corrida's room...)

Japanese: （どうしても、このろうかを 通らなければならないわけか）
English: (He would have had to pass through this hallway.)

Japanese: トイレなら、それぞれの 部屋にあるんだけど、
English: There are toilets in each room in this hotel,

Japanese: このホテルでは、いろいろな イベントが開かれるので、
English: but since all sorts of events are held here,

Japanese: 泊まっている客以外の人たちが 使うのだろう。
English: there are bathrooms for people who aren't staying here to use as well.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくもそのひとりだけど。
English: Perfect for people like me, who can't afford a stay here in the first place...

Japanese: 向かいの花輪にくらべて、 さらにゴージャスだ。
English: Compared to the flowers on the other side of the hall, these are much more gorgeous.

Japanese: 事務所、レコード会社、 ファンクラブ、兄弟一同‥‥
English: Let's see... Record companies, fan clubs, company workers, family...

Japanese: 持って帰るのが タイヘンそうだな。
English: Carrying all these flowers home would be hard, I think...

Japanese: そう思って、ウチが ちょっぴり、手伝っといたワ。
English: Yeah, that's what I thought too, so I was a nice enough gal to lend a helpin' hand!

Japanese: ‥‥ナイショで。
English: ...Secretly, of course.

Japanese: （いつか、ベンゴすることに なりそうだな、このヒト‥‥）
English: (I have a feeling one of these days, I'm going to end up defending her in court...)

Japanese: “藤見野　イサオ様　控え室” 張り紙にはそう書かれている。
English: There's a piece of paper taped to the door that says, "Juan Corrida's Room".

Japanese: そこがサツジン現場、 というワケや。
English: So that's the scene of the murder, I reckon.

Japanese: ナニかが起こる、とは 思っとったけど、
English: I thought somethin' had gone on that night,

Japanese: まさかサツジンとはなー。
English: but I didn't never think it was murder.

Japanese: あの‥‥ カメラがなくなったんですか？
English: So you lost your camera...?

Japanese: タダのカメラやないで。 定価１６万円の最高級品やッ！
English: It ain't no ordinary camera! Ya buy it in a store and it's $1,600 brand new!

Japanese: おや。‥‥たしか先ほどは、 ７万円のカメラ、と‥‥
English: Huh? But didn't you just say you bought it for $700?

Japanese: 店員とハラぁ割って ハナシ合ったんや。５時間。
English: I had a nice long talk with the guy at the store... About five hours, I reckon.

Japanese: カメラのちっちゃなキズやら、 店員のタイドのワルさやら‥‥
English: I made this itty-bitty scratch on it and the manager got all huffed up in the face...

Japanese: ちくちくジックリ言い聞かせたら、 マケてくれたワケ。ナミダ流して。
English: He gave me his talkin' to and was real mean about it too. He done made me cry at that!

Japanese: そのカメラが‥‥いつ、 なくなったのですか？
English: When did you lose your camera?

Japanese: ゆんべ、事件があったアト。
English: Last night, after the murder happened.

Japanese: 取り調べやらナニやら、 ゴタついとったそのとき！
English: Musta been when I was busy runnin' around lookin' into things!

Japanese: ウチの最高級のアイボーは‥‥ 消えっちまったんやッ！
English: That's when I lost sight of my dear, darlin' expensive sweetie!

Japanese: ナツミさん、その最高級品で 何を撮影してたんですか？
English: Lotta, what did you capture with that expensive camera of yours?

Japanese: さあ‥‥。目についたモノ、 テケトーに撮ってたからなァ。
English: I don't rightly know. I snapped a shot of anythin' that caught my eye.

Japanese: よくおぼえてへん。 ‥‥スクープは、なかったワ。
English: So I don't 'member. ...And 'sides, I couldn't get anythin' for my big scoop.

Japanese: （消えたカメラ、か。 事件に関係あるのかな‥‥？）
English: (I wonder if Lotta's missing camera is even connected to the murder...?)

Japanese: 証拠品《ナツミのカメラ》のデータ を法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Lotta's Camera added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ナツミさん、事件があった ころのコト、聞かせてください。
English: Lotta, please tell me what you know about what happened at the time of the murder.

Japanese: ゆんべはナ。授賞式が始まる前から このヘン、ウロついとったワ。
English: Well, from before the ceremony last night, I was hangin' around here in this area.

Japanese: せやなー、王都楼がタイホされる コロまで、おったで。
English: Yeah, actually I was here until around the time Mr. Engarde was arrested.

Japanese: ‥‥何してたんですか？ こんなところで。
English: What were you doing here...?

Japanese: 決まってるやん！
English: You sure you went to school, City Boy?

Japanese: ナツミさんの行くところ、 スクープあり、や！
English: Wherever Lotta Hart goes, there's a story to be found! A big scoop to be had!

Japanese: スクープ？
English: A big scoop...?

Japanese: 言うたやん。ウチは今、バリバリ いやらしい芸能カメラマンなんや。
English: I told ya before. I'm gonna be the best tabloid photographer the world's ever seen!

Japanese: メチャロン、狙ってたでえ！ シャッター・チャンス！
English: Reckon course that means I'm always lookin' for the perfect shot!

Japanese: （“スクープ”‥‥って、 なんのコトだろう‥‥）
English: (I wonder what "scoop" she was after this time...?)

Japanese: ‥‥ま。いろんなスターさんに 目ウツリしとったから、
English: Although, I was also on the look out for the other stars that were here,

Japanese: ずっとここにいた‥‥という ワケでもないねんケド。
English: so... maybe I wasn't here the entire time...

Japanese: ナツミさん‥‥ここでずっと、 見張ってたんですよね？
English: Lotta... Are you sure you weren't here the entire time?

Japanese: スクープ写真を撮ろうと。
English: So you could take a picture for your big scoop?

Japanese: せや。 ジャーナリストっぽいやろ？
English: Well... Maybe I was. But that's what real journalists do!

Japanese: ウラ情報があったからナ。 決定的な写真、撮ったろ思ォて。
English: I got some juicy inside info, so I thought to myself, why not get a picture for proof?

Japanese: いったい、 どんなスクープを‥‥？
English: What kind of story was it that you would hang around here...?

Japanese: おっとォ、そりゃアレや。 企業ヒミツってヤツ？
English: Oops, sorry, Mr. Lawyer. Can't be tellin' you that. Trade secret, ya know?

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥。また何か かくすつもりか‥‥）
English: (Not again... Why does everyone have something to hide...?)

Japanese: 困りましたね‥‥。
English: We've been stopped, haven't we?

Japanese: はは。やった。困ってはる！
English: Ha ha, yeah! Take that, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: （“これぞ生きがい”みたいな、 とてもサワヤカな笑顔だ）
English: (I'm glad someone around here is happy... Ms. Lotta and your "I rule!" smile...)

Japanese: ゆうべ、ナツミさんが 狙っていたスクープ、って‥‥
English: You were on the hunt for your big scoop last night?

Japanese: つまらんゴシップ写真や。 サツジンとはカンケーない。
English: It's just a bunch of tabloid photos. They ain't got nothing to do with the murder.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥サイコ・ロックを 解除するしかありませんね。
English: It looks like we have no choice but to remove the Psyche-Locks.

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥。
English: Yeah... I guess so.

Japanese: イサオさんと霧緒さんの スキャンダルを？
English: You were looking into Mr. Corrida and Ms. Andrews' affair, weren't you?

Japanese: せや！　２人の密会を スクープするつもりやったんや！
English: You got it! I was gonna get myself a scoop by catchin' 'em in a secret meetin'!

Japanese: でも、もうすでに週刊誌の 記事になってるんですよ。
English: But there's already an article about it in one of the weekly tabloid magazines...

Japanese: 今さら、スクープもないでしょう。
English: It's no longer breaking news.

Japanese: ナニ言うとるんやッ！
English: Whatcha just say!?

Japanese: 大衆が求めてるのは“Ｋ．Ｋ” なんてアイマイなモンと、ちゃう！
English: Her initials are "A. A." What kind of vague thing is that!?

Japanese: ケッテー的な証拠！　そのもの ズバリ、写真が見たいんやッ！
English: That ain't no proof of nothin'! People are gonna wanna see real proof!

Japanese: ‥‥少なくとも、ウチはそうや。
English: Well, at least I do. So that's what I was doin'! Getting photos!

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: ワザとちょっとピンボケにして、 読者のキョーミをそそる。
English: I'm gonna whip up the reader's interest with some gossip and a little misleadin'.

Japanese: それに、ウチがテケトーにハナシを でっち上げて、特ダネの完成や！
English: Then spice it up a little and have myself an exclusive story!

Japanese: （ヒドいジャーナリスト‥‥）
English: (Wow, Lotta, nice journalistic integrity you got there...)

Japanese: ウチ、もうデッチ上げる ハナシも作っとったんや。
English: I already finished writin' up my spicy article, ya know.

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: そのメモも、 どっか行ってしもたんや。
English: The paper I wrote it on, my note to myself, it's gone.

Japanese: めも、ですか‥‥
English: Your note to yourself?

Japanese: １６万円のカメラのケースに 入れといたからなァ。
English: It was inside the case of my $1,600 camera.

Japanese: いっしょに なくなってしもたワ。
English: They done run off together.

Japanese: ウチはスクープを撮りに来たんや！ お宝を吐き出すため、ちゃうでェ！
English: I came here for my big story! Didn't come 'ere to have my treasure disappear on me!

Japanese: そ、そうなんでしょうね‥‥
English: Y-Yeah, I understand...

Japanese: ハラ立つわー。ウチのモットーは “むしり撮れ！　シリの毛までも”
English: It's enough to make a gal go bonkers, I tell ya! What's with people now, anyway!?

Japanese: ‥‥やのになあ。
English: I never thought I'd see the day when someone done steal somethin' from me.

Japanese: ナツミさんのメモ、か‥‥。
English: You really want that note back, huh...?

Japanese: （根も葉もミもフタもユメもキボー もないデッチ上げなんだろうな）
English: (I've got no idea why though. The story on that note is probably a bold faced lie...)

Japanese: 証拠品《ナツミのカメラ》のデータ を書きなおした。
English: Lotta's Camera updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: あ。なんやソレ。 ‥‥キシャカイケン‥‥？
English: Hey, what's that? ...A press conference ticket?

Japanese: ウチは聞いてへんで！
English: How come I ain't got myself one of them!?

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくに怒らないで くださいよ‥‥
English: P-Please don't direct your anger at me...

Japanese: どーして、このナツミさんを ショータイせえへんのや！
English: Why in tarnations wasn't I invited to that!?

Japanese: アンタみたいな、しがない大衆の 底に埋もれたチリなんて、
English: How come someone on the bottom of the popularity chart like you got the info,

Japanese: ショータイされる価値、 ゼロやでゼロ！　なあ、そーやろ！
English: but not me!? Info like that is worth nothin' to ya, am I right or what!?

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくはそのテイドの ニンゲンだったのか‥‥）
English: (...Is that the kind of person she thinks I am...?)

Japanese: な、なるほどくん！ そんなに落ちこまないでください！
English: Are you going to stand there and take that, Mr. Nick!?

Japanese: ううう‥‥買ったばっかり やったのに‥‥。
English: Nngh... And I just bought that camera...

Japanese: いったい、ダレやッ！ ちょろまかしたヤツぁ！
English: Who did it!? The thievin' rascal!

Japanese: ぼくの目をまっすぐ見て 言わないでくださいよ‥‥
English: Please don't look straight at me while you're saying that...

Japanese: 見つけしだい、射殺やッ！
English: When I find the bugger who went and done this, they're gonna pay with a bullet!

Japanese: （ベンゴは引き受けないで おこう‥‥）
English: (And if you do that, don't expect me to defend you...)

Japanese: あの。何か情報を‥‥
English: Um... Do you know anything...

Japanese: 何度も言わせんといて。 ウチは、ジャーナリストや。
English: I've told ya how many times now? I'm a journalist.

Japanese: 見さかいなくジョーホー タレ流すワケ、ないやんか！
English: I've got so much info runnin' laps in my head I forget things here and there!

Japanese: そ、そんなに怒らなくても‥‥
English: Please don't get so worked up over this...

Japanese: ハッ！ しょんぼりしてもムダやッ！
English: Hah! I don't care if ya beg me!

Japanese: “コドモに特にキビしく！” ‥‥ナツミさんの教育方針や。
English: "Be extra tough on kids!" That's this gal's motto on learnin' youngsters.

Japanese: いやー、ゴメンなぁ。
English: Ah, just wanna say sorry to ya.

Japanese: はい？
English: For...?

Japanese: ウチ、ぜーったいアンタが ハンニンや、って思っとったんや。
English: Well, I really, absolutely thought you were the murderer.
...Huh?

Japanese: ホレ。こないだの事件。 真宵はんの、アレ。
English: You know... Last time, that thing with Maya Fey...

Japanese: どど、どうしてですか！
English: Wh-Wh-What!? But why!?

Japanese: だって、盛り上がるやろ？ そのほうが。
English: I mean, you were all worked up, ya know?

Japanese: うう‥‥ヒドい。
English: *sniffle* ...Meanie...

Japanese: ナツミさん、 アナーキーすぎますよ！
English: Lotta, that was very insensitive of you!

Japanese: ‥‥アレ。そういえば、 今日はいっしょじゃないんやね。
English: Now that I think about it, she isn't with y'all today, is she...?
Um...

Japanese: アカンで。目ェはなしたら。
English: She's a spunky one! You shouldn't have been lettin' her outta yer sight.

Japanese: 何しでかすか、わからんから なあ、このコ。
English: Who knows what she's up to now...

Japanese: （ヒトのコト、 言えないと思うけど）
English: (Like you're one to talk...)

Japanese: いやー、コイツは やっぱり、アレやな。
English: She's got one of them personalities like she's...

Japanese: ムチはアカン。 ムチャアカン。
English: "whipped" without her "whip"!

Japanese: ‥‥どうや？　今の！ ちょっとシャレとるやろ？
English: Ha ha, how's that!? A pun worthy of a joke book, yeah?

Japanese: ‥‥聞いてませんでした。
English: ...Sorry, I zoned out.

Japanese: なんや、アマいなー。 何度でも聞かしたるで。
English: Ah, well, I'll forgive the wax in yer ears this once and tell it to ya one more time.

Japanese: ああ、このオバハンなぁ。
English: This old granny?

Japanese: なんかウチに目ェつけて、 ナンクセつけて来たんや。
English: Ah, she kept on glarin' at me with those eyes of hers. As if it's my fault.

Japanese: “イサオちゃんのスクープなんて、 ユルさないよォ！”‥‥なんて。
English: Kept babblin' how she won't forgive people who write "trash" about her Juan...

Japanese: ‥‥ありゃア、ちょっとした シュラバやったワ。
English: ...Ah, yeah, we sorta got into a little fight, ya know...

Japanese: （その場にいなくて ホントによかった‥‥）
English: (A "little" fight? Knowing those two, it was probably more of a blabbering contest.)

Japanese: ウチ、ヒーローやらなんやら、 そんなのはどーでもええねん。
English: I don't really care for 'em "heroes" or whatnot.

Japanese: そうなのですか‥‥。
English: Oh, I see...

Japanese: お子さま向けのアマっちょろい ドラマなんて、つまらん！
English: And shows they got for kids, those adventures are just plain ol' borin'!

Japanese: オトナは、もっとドロドロした、 なまぐさーいネタを求めてるんや！
English: Now, adults. We get more caught up in messy, sticky, real situations!

Japanese: ‥‥少なくとも、ウチはそうや。
English: ...Even me...

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: O-Oh...

Japanese: おじょうちゃんも、今にわかる！
English: Sounds like the tyke's gettin' what I'm goin' with this!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと！　ヘンなコト 吹きこまないでくださいよ！
English: W-Wait a sec here! Don't go around getting her mixed up in things like that!

Japanese: このヘアスタイルなー、 けっこう手入れがタイヘンなんや。
English: This kind of hairstyle is pretty hard to get, ya know. Hard to care for too.

Japanese: そうそう。こないだ、 アタマん中、まさぐっとったら、
English: Just the other day, I ran my hand through my hair to fix it up,

Japanese: １００円、出てきたんや。 たまにはいいコトあるなー。
English: and suddenly, out pops this dollar coin! That's gotta be like hittin' the jackpot!

Japanese: わあ。いいですねえ。
English: Wow, that's really lucky!

Japanese: （スゴい会話だな‥‥）
English: (*sigh* What a deep conversation that was...)

Japanese: ～スクープ～
English: -- Big Scoop --

Japanese: ‥‥ナツミさん。 聞かせてもらいましょうか。
English: Lotta, will you please answer my questions?

Japanese: 事件の夜、なぜ被害者の控え室を 見張っていたのか。
English: On the night of the murder, why were you loitering around the victim's room?

Japanese: だから、言うたやろ？ スクープ、狙っとったんや！
English: I told ya, didn't I? For my scoop!

Japanese: ぼくが聞きたいのは、 そのスクープの内容です。
English: What I want to know about are the details of this "scoop".

Japanese: そ‥‥それはチョット、 言われへんなァ。
English: Th-That's not somethin' I can tell ya.

Japanese: ホレ、ウチ、そいつで おまんま食べてるワケやし‥‥。
English: I mean, that there's my bread and butter...

Japanese: いやらしい芸能カメラマンが 狙う、スクープ‥‥
English: Alright then. An unpleasant tabloid photographer looking for a scoop...

Japanese: それって、もしかして‥‥ スキャンダルですか？
English: I'm going to say that you were looking into a scandal.

Japanese: うッ‥‥！
English: Grr!

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ナツミさんが スクープしようとしていたのは‥‥
English: Could it be that you, Lotta Hart, were looking for a break with a huge story?

Japanese: 被害者のイサオさんと、この人物の スキャンダルだったのでは？
English: Perhaps an unfolding scandal between Juan Corrida and this person...?

Japanese: ‥‥ダレや、それ。
English: Who the heck is that...?
Um...

Japanese: ‥‥ジャーナリストを バカにしちゃアカンで。
English: You think us journalists are all a bunch of idiots...? Ya can't fool me!

Japanese: ウチはこれでもプロやッ！
English: I'm a pro!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥そうですか。
English: O-Oh, really...?

Japanese: ‥‥で。ダレ、それ？
English: So... Who's this person anyway...?

Japanese: （ジャーナリストなら 自分で調べろよ！）
English: (If you're such a pro, why don't you do your own research and figure it out!)

Japanese: ‥‥と、とにかく。 もう一度、聞きますよ。
English: Um, let's try that again, shall we...?

Japanese: こ、コイツは‥‥
English: Th-This woman...

Japanese: ‥‥華宮　霧緒さん。 王都楼　真悟のマネージャーです。
English: She's Adrian Andrews, Matt Engarde's manager.

Japanese: うぐぐ‥‥ッ！
English: Mmph!

Japanese: “トノサマンのマネージャー、 ライバルの忍者ナンジャと密会”
English: The Nickel Samurai's manager, caught secretly meeting with his rival, the Jammin' Ninja.

Japanese: ‥‥スクープのネタとしては、 最高ですよね。
English: It would be the hottest story of the season, wouldn't it?

Japanese: た、たいしたヤマカンやなァ ‥‥ニイちゃん。
English: You're pretty good at this guessin' thing, Mr. Lawyer.

Japanese: だけどな。カンだけで スクープはモノにでけへんのや。
English: But ya can't just make up any ol' thing and think it'll make the papers.

Japanese: コンキョがないとなァ。
English: Ya gotta have backup.

Japanese: コンキョ‥‥
English: Backup...?

Japanese: せやせや。‥‥なんつったっけ。 ‥‥“じゅーす・しょうゆ”‥‥？
English: Yeah, yeah. Ya gotta have that... that what's it... "newsauce"...?

Japanese: ‥‥もしかして、 ニュース・ソースのことですか？
English: ...Um, you mean, "news source"...?

Japanese: それやッ！
English: That's it!

Japanese: イサオと華宮　霧緒がアヤしい っていうコンキョ、見せてみィ！
English: So show me somethin' that shows that Juan guy had somethin' with Ms. Andrews!

Japanese: アンタ、ウチのコト バカにしとんのか？
English: You really think I'm some kind of idiot, don'tcha!?

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: プロはコンキョがなきゃ動かん、 て言うとるんやで？
English: Don't ya know pros don't go for a lead without proof it's somethin' worth going after?

Japanese: ナツミさんなら、コレぐらいでも 動くんじゃないかな、と。
English: Well, if the pro we're talking about is you, then this looks right up your alley...

Japanese: 動くかいイイッ！
English: As if!

Japanese: （シッパイか‥‥）
English: (Looks like I touched a nerve there...)

Japanese: エエかァ。ウチにはレッキとした ジュース・ショーユがあったんや！
English: Now listen here, City Boy. Show me a good, solid newsauce or else!

Japanese: これは、週刊誌の記事です。
English: This is the article from a certain weekly tabloid.

Japanese: “忍者ナンジャ、深夜の密会！”
English: "Jammin' Midnight Rendezvous!?"

Japanese: “お相手は‥‥ マネージャーのＫ．Ｋか？”
English: "...to the mysterious, yet beautiful manager to the stars, Ms. A. A.!"

Japanese: ‥‥うほっ！
English: ...Aaah!

Japanese: イサオさんに、マネージャーは ついていないそうです。
English: Mr. Corrida didn't have a manager of his own.

Japanese: しかし！　そのライバル、 オートロさんのマネージャーは‥‥
English: What's more, his rival, Mr. Engarde's manager... Adrian Andrews...

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒‥‥ イニシャルはＫ．Ｋです！
English: She has the initials A. A.!

Japanese: あなたはこの記事を見て、 証拠写真を撮ろうと考えた。
English: You saw this article and then thought to take some pictures of them as proof.

Japanese: だからイサオさんの控え室を 見張っていたんです！
English: That's why you were lurking around Mr. Corrida's door last night!

Japanese: うがわわああッ！
English: Waaaaaah!!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 控え室前　ろうか
English: March 21 Gatewater Hotel Hallway

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。手がかりはだいぶ 集まったね。
English: Well, I think we've gathered about all we can...

Japanese: でも、真宵さまは‥‥ だいじょうぶでしょうか？
English: Wh-What about Mystic Maya? Is she alright...?

Japanese: （さすがに春美ちゃん、 ツラそうだな‥‥）
English: (Oh, Pearls... She looks so worn out by all this...)

Japanese: （ぜんぜん寝てないし、 １日中、歩きまわって‥‥）
English: (She hasn't slept at all, and has been walking all over the place with me today...)

Japanese: どうされましたか？ なるほどくん。
English: What's wrong, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: いっぺん、事務所へ帰ろうか。 つかれたでしょ？
English: Let's go back to the office for a little while. You're really tired, right?

Japanese: あ。いえ、そんな！
English: Oh, no!

Japanese: わたくしはべつに ‥‥ゲンキです、ゲンキ。
English: I'm OK. Really! I'm fine. I really am.

Japanese: （そうは見えないんだけど）
English: (You don't look fine to me...)

Japanese: 楽屋の前に花があるのは ふつうだけど、
English: I can understand flowers in front of the dressing rooms,

Japanese: なんでこんなところに クマのぬいぐるみが‥‥？
English: but what are stuffed bears doing here...?

Japanese: もしかして、クマが大好きな アクション・スターなのか‥‥？
English: Could it be that there's an action star who has a soft spot for teddy bears...?

Japanese: ‥‥まさか、ね。
English: ...Nah, can't be.

Japanese: 向かいの花輪にくらべて、 さらにゴージャスだ。
English: Compared to the flowers on the other side of the hall, these are much more gorgeous.

Japanese: 事務所、レコード会社、 ファンクラブ、兄弟一同‥‥
English: Let's see... Record companies, fan clubs, company workers, family...

Japanese: 持って帰るのが タイヘンそうだな。
English: Carrying all these flowers home would be hard, I think...

Japanese: “藤見野　イサオ様　控え室” 張り紙にはそう書かれている。
English: There's a piece of paper taped to the door that says, "Juan Corrida's Room".

Japanese: “フジミノ　イサオ”。 ‥‥たしか、スターの名前だ。
English: "Juan Corrida"... His name just sounds like a star's name.

Japanese: 聞いたことはあるけど、 よく知らないな。
English: I've heard it before, but I don't know anything about him at all.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 王都楼　真悟の控え室
English: March 21 Gatewater Hotel Engarde's Hotel Room

Japanese: あの‥‥ここは？
English: Um... Where are we?

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟の控え室。 ‥‥ぼくたちの依頼人の部屋だよ。
English: We're in Mr. Matt Engarde's dressing room... This is our client's room.

Japanese: 何か、ご用ですか。
English: May I help you with something?

Japanese: え。あ、あの。ぼくたちは‥‥
English: Um, uh... We're...

Japanese: 王都楼の弁護士ですね。 ‥‥そんなことはわかっています。
English: You're Mr. Engarde's lawyer, correct? I gathered as much.

Japanese: 私のほうでも探していましたが、 ムダになったようです。
English: I also looked for lawyers on my end, but to no avail.

Japanese: え‥‥。 ど、どうして弁護士だと‥‥？
English: Um... How did you know I'm his lawyer...?

Japanese: 先ほど、“ぼくたちの依頼人”と おっしゃいました。
English: You were just saying that he is your client.

Japanese: この状況で“依頼人”という コトバを使う人物‥‥弁護士です。
English: In a situation like this, the only person who would use such a word would be his lawyer.

Japanese: ‥‥すごい。
English: Wow...

Japanese: 当然のことです。
English: It's simple deduction, really.

Japanese: 開廷は明日。王都楼の状況は、 とても楽観できるものではない。
English: The trial is tomorrow, and Mr. Engarde's situation is looking rather grim.

Japanese: あなたは、手がかりを求めて 走り回っている‥‥ちがいますか？
English: So you came here, one stop in your mad dash, to find clues to build his case, correct?

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥当たらずとも 遠からず、といったところです。
English: Well, you're not totally right, but you're not totally off either.

Japanese: なるほどくん。　ミエをはっている 場合ではありません。
English: It's really not the time to be showing off, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: 私、華宮　霧緒（かみやきりお）と 申します。
English: I am Adrian Andrews.

Japanese: ムダなことがキライなので‥‥ 用件を、どうぞ。
English: I hate to waste time, so let's get down to business.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: A-Alright...

Japanese: （からだは小さいのに、 堂々としたもんだな‥‥）
English: (She may be of small stature, but appearances can be deceiving...)

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろいいでしょうか。 仕事がありますから。
English: Well, that's enough for now. I still have work to do, so...

Japanese: はい。
English: I understand.

Japanese: １つだけ‥‥おねがいがあります。
English: Oh, I have one small favor to ask.

Japanese: 私のコト‥‥自殺未遂のこと、 ヒミツにしてほしいんです。
English: My... attempted suicide... I'd like for you to keep it a secret.

Japanese: きりおさん‥‥
English: Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: 私がその‥‥弱いって、 みんなに知られたらと思うと‥‥
English: If... If people found out about my weakness...

Japanese: それなら‥‥いっそ、私、 死を選んだほうがマシです。
English: I... I would sooner choose to die than live.

Japanese: わわわわ、わかりました！ ぼくたちだけのヒミツにします。
English: A-A-Alright! I understand! We'll keep it a secret!

Japanese: （霧緒さん‥‥思いつめる タイプなのか‥‥）
English: (Ms. Andrews... I guess she's the "always thinking" type...)

Japanese: （ウカツなことは言えないな‥‥）
English: (She never says anything carelessly, it seems...)

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you very much.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。 ちょっと、よろしいですか？
English: Mr. Nick... Can I ask you something...?

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: What is it?

Japanese: きりおさん‥‥先ほどからずっと、 カードをいじっていますよね‥‥？
English: Ms. Andrews has been playing with that card in her hand since a little while back...

Japanese: （カード‥‥たしかに‥‥）
English: (That card...? Yeah, I guess she has...)

Japanese: 霧緒さん。 そのカード‥‥なんですか？
English: Ms. Andrews, what is that card you're holding...?

Japanese: え？　あ、ああ‥‥これですか？
English: Huh? O-Oh, this?

Japanese: さあ‥‥いつのまにか、 バッグに入っていたもので‥‥
English: I don't quite know. It just suddenly appeared in my handbag...

Japanese: なんだこりゃ。 ‥‥サザエ、ですか？
English: What is it? It looks like... A seashell?

Japanese: そうみたいですね‥‥。
English: That's what it looks like, doesn't it?

Japanese: おぼえがないんです。 どこかで拾ったのかしら‥‥？
English: I honestly don't remember owning this card... I wonder where I picked it up from...?

Japanese: （キオクがハッキリしない、か。 めずらしいこともあるもんだ）
English: (Her not remembering something clearly? Sounds like it would be a rare occurrence.)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、王都楼さんのこと、 よろしくおねがいします。
English: Well, I must be off. I leave Mr. Engarde in your "capable" hands.

Japanese: 食事のあとが‥‥ふたりぶん。
English: Looks like dishes left over from dinner... A dinner for two, at that.

Japanese: きりおさんと、オートロさんの ぶんですね、きっと。
English: I'm sure they're Mr. Engarde and Ms. Andrews' plates.

Japanese: メニューは‥‥ホネつきステーキ だったみたいだな。
English: Looks like they had T-bone steaks... What's with Global Studios and T-bone steaks...?

Japanese: オートロさんのトランクだろう。
English: It's probably Mr. Engarde's suitcase.

Japanese: 授賞式に出るだけなのに、 たいそうなニモツだな。
English: For someone who was only going to be here for the award show, this is a lot of stuff.

Japanese: 着替えが３日ぶんぐらいあるぞ。
English: Looks like he has about three days' worth of clothes in here.

Japanese: やっぱり、すたーさんは ちがいますねえ‥‥。
English: Stars really are different from us, aren't they...?

Japanese: 皿に、でっかいホネが 乗っている。
English: There's a giant bone sitting on the plate.

Japanese: ‥‥わたくし、おニクは ニガテです‥‥。 I...
English: I don't really like meat...

Japanese: （こっちの皿‥‥なんか ヘンな気がするけど‥‥）
English: (There's something... weird about this plate...)

Japanese: （どうしてだろう‥‥？）
English: (I just wish I could put my finger on what it is...)

Japanese: ソファに、和服がかかっている。
English: There's some samurai-looking clothes on the sofa here.

Japanese: ハッピですね、あれは。
English: Um, I think that jacket looking thing is called a "happi".

Japanese: （おみやげに 持って帰りたいな‥‥）
English: (Whatever it is, I'm sure something like that would make a great souvenir.)

Japanese: （きっと、真宵ちゃんが よろこぶだろう‥‥）
English: (Maya would be absolutely thrilled...)

Japanese: 向こうはベッドルームだ。
English: That's the bedroom over there.

Japanese: あれが“べっど” というものですか‥‥
English: That's a bed...?

Japanese: わたくし、初めて見ました。
English: Wow, they have really big beds here!

Japanese: まず必要になるデータは、 事件当夜の関係者の行動です。
English: I assume the first thing you need to know is what everyone was doing that night, correct?

Japanese: は、はい。そうですね‥‥。
English: Y-Yes, that is correct.

Japanese: では、さっそくお話しします。
English: Then I will tell you.

Japanese: 授賞式が始まる前、私は 王都楼と食事をとりました。
English: Before the award ceremony, I had dinner with Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: ‥‥この控え室で。
English: In this very room, I might add.

Japanese: “食事”‥‥。あの、 何を食べたんですか？
English: "Dinner"? What did you eat?

Japanese: ‥‥言ったはずです。 ムダな会話はキライだ、と。
English: I told you. I hate to waste time with trifling details.

Japanese: そんなこと、テーブルを見れば 聞くまでもありません。
English: If you took a look at the table yourself, you wouldn't need to ask me.

Japanese: （‥‥キビしいヒトだな‥‥）
English: (...I bet she's a lot of fun at parties...)

Japanese: 授賞式が始まってからは、 会場のほうへ。
English: When the award show was starting, I headed for Viola Hall.

Japanese: 式が終わってからは、 この控え室に‥‥？
English: And after the show ended, you came back to this room...?

Japanese: いいえ。私には、こまごました 仕事がありましたから。
English: No, I had some small errands to run.

Japanese: ロビーで、準備を 手伝っていました。
English: I helped with the preparations in the lobby.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。 （受賞記念のショーかな）
English: Oh... (Preparations for the post- ceremony show, I guess...)

Japanese: アトラクション・ショーの時間に、 王都楼を呼びに来ました。
English: When it was time for the post- ceremony show, I came back to call for Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: そのついでに、藤見野　イサオさん の控え室ものぞいたのですが‥‥
English: After that, I went to visit Mr. Corrida...

Japanese: そこで、 死体を発見したわけですね。
English: And that's when you found his body, isn't it?

Japanese: はあ‥‥シッカリした かたですねえ。
English: You really held strong through everything...

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥スキのない ヒトだな）
English: (Yeah... She does seem to be mentally tough as nails...)

Japanese: ‥‥あの。ところで、 あなたは‥‥？
English: Um, so... About you and...

Japanese: ‥‥あきれた。
English: Stop right there.

Japanese: そんなコトも知らずに、 今まで私と会話を？
English: Don't expect me to believe you have been talking to me with- out knowing how we relate.

Japanese: す、すみません‥‥。
English: S-Sorry...

Japanese: なぜ王都楼があなたを 選んだのか、リカイできません。
English: I have no idea why Mr. Engarde chose you as his lawyer.

Japanese: （‥‥なんでこんなコト 言われなきゃならないんだよ）
English: (Why did she have to go and say something like that...?)

Japanese: なるほどくん、ガマンです。
English: Mr. Nick... Calm down and hang in there.

Japanese: あとでわたくし、肩をたたいて さしあげますから。
English: I'll give you a shoulder rub to relieve your stress later, alright?

Japanese: 名前は先ほど申し上げました。 ‥‥王都楼のマネージャーです。
English: I already gave you my name earlier, but I'll add that I'm Mr. Engarde's manager.

Japanese: マネージャー‥‥
English: His manager...

Japanese: そういえば、被害者‥‥イサオさん のマネージャーは‥‥？
English: Speaking of managers, did the victim, Mr. Corrida have one?

Japanese: いません。
English: No, he did not.

Japanese: いない？
English: He didn't?

Japanese: 私ども英都プロダクションと ちがって、光映プロには、
English: Global Studios has a very different policy from Worldwide Studios in that

Japanese: 役者１人ひとりにマネージャーを つけるよゆうなど、ありません。
English: Worldwide Studios does not assign individual managers to their stars.

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: この業界も、 なかなか苦しいのです。
English: This industry is very ruthless and unforgiving.

Japanese: 見ればあなたも、ずいぶん助手には お困りのようですね。
English: Actually, you look quite ruthless and unforgiving yourself to your poor partner.

Japanese: そんな小さな子を連れまわして。
English: Dragging a little girl like her to places like this. Honestly.

Japanese: まあ！　わた、わたくしは 真宵さまのために‥‥！
English: You're wrong! I... I'm doing this to help Mystic Maya...!

Japanese: 春美ちゃん、ガマンして。 あとでジュース買ってあげるから。
English: Pearls... Calm down and hang in there. I'll buy you a juice later, alright?

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Nngh...

Japanese: 何か、心当たりは ありませんか？
English: Do you have any ideas?

Japanese: ‥‥なんのことでしょうか。
English: As to...?

Japanese: なぜ、イサオさんが 殺されたのか？　その理由です。
English: As to why Mr. Corrida was murdered?

Japanese: どうしてそんなことを、 私が‥‥？
English: Why would you ask me about such a thing...?

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくが聞いてるんです。 心当たりはありませんか？
English: I'm just doing my job. So, do you have any ideas?

Japanese: き、霧緒さん‥‥？
English: M-Ms. Andrews...?

Japanese: ザンネンですが‥‥特に、 お話しできることはありません。
English: Sorry, but there's nothing more I have to add to this conversation.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 サイコ・ロック、ですか‥‥？
English: Is it a Psyche-Lock, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: うん。‥‥ますます こんがらがってきたよ。
English: Yes... There's getting to be more and more of these lately.

Japanese: イサオさんが殺害された ことについて‥‥
English: You know something, don't you?

Japanese: 何か、ご存じなんですね？
English: About why Mr. Corrida was murdered...

Japanese: ‥‥どうしてそんなことが？
English: Why are you asking me?

Japanese: あなたには、私のココロが 見えると言うのですか？
English: Don't tell me you can see inside my heart.

Japanese: （見えるんだよな‥‥それが）
English: (No, but I can see the locks you have on your psyche.)

Japanese: ロックがある以上、 解除しないかぎりは‥‥
English: If you don't remove her locks...

Japanese: （聞いてもムダ、ってことか）
English: (Yeah, it's worthless to even ask...)

Japanese: たしかに‥‥私は、ひとりでは 不安で生きていけない女よ。
English: It's true. I am a woman who can only live in insecurity.

Japanese: からだも小さくて‥‥それで、 よけい自分に自信が持てなくて。
English: I'm physically small, and I don't really have a lot of self-confidence.

Japanese: ‥‥それを押しかくして、 強いフリをして生きてきた。
English: I've pushed against all that, though... I've tried to live strongly.

Japanese: ‥‥知られたくなかった‥‥
English: I never wanted anyone to find out the truth...

Japanese: きりおさん‥‥
English: Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: このことだけは、だれにも 知られたくなかった‥‥
English: This one thing... It's the one thing I wanted to take with me to the grave...

Japanese: 私だけの‥‥ ヒミツだったのに‥‥
English: It was my secret. Mine and mine alone.

Japanese: ‥‥すみません。
English: I... I'm sorry.

Japanese: あなたを見くびっていたようね。
English: You probably think I'm a worthless human being right now, don't you?

Japanese: 聞かせてください、霧緒さん。 ‥‥本当のことを。
English: Please, Ms. Andrews. All I want to know is the truth.

Japanese: 由利恵さんが亡くなって、 遺書がかくされた、って聞いたわ。
English: After Celeste passed away, I heard that someone had hidden her suicide note...

Japanese: ‥‥藤見野　イサオが かくしてしまった、って‥‥
English: And that someone was Juan Corrida.

Japanese: ‥‥由利恵さん‥‥ 私、あの人がいなければ‥‥
English: Celeste... Without her...

Japanese: こ、怖くて‥‥。みんなが私の 敵に見えてしまって‥‥
English: Without her, I became scared. Everything... Everyone seemed like they were out to get me.

Japanese: その遺書を手に入れるため、 あなたは彼に近づいたんですね。
English: So you got close to Mr. Corrida to recover her suicide note, correct?

Japanese: どうしても、その雑誌の記事を 真実に仕立てあげたいみたいね。
English: Looks like that tabloid reported the truth after all. Ironic, isn't it?

Japanese: 火のないところにケムリは立たない ‥‥って言いますからね。
English: Well, like they say, where there's smoke, there's fire.

Japanese: そこにムリヤリ火をつけて 焼きつくすのが、芸能界よ。
English: And if they purposely add fuel to the fire, they keep the celebrity world burning.

Japanese: 遺書？　‥‥そんなもののために、 私は殺人なんてしません。
English: But as for the suicide note? I didn't and wouldn't kill anyone for it.

Japanese: ‥‥ワリに合いませんから。
English: It just doesn't suit me, that's all.

Japanese: この記者会見の手配は、 あなたが？
English: You were the one taking care of the arrangements for this press conference, right?

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。
English: Yes, that's right.

Japanese: でも‥‥オートロさん、 “知らない”って‥‥
English: But Mr. Engarde said he "didn't know" about it.

Japanese: そうなのですか？
English: Is that what he said...?
Huh...?

Japanese: じつは私も、ハッキリしたことは 知らなかったのです。
English: Actually, I didn't know all the details either.

Japanese: ‥‥宣伝部のほうから たのまれたので、
English: It was a request from the publicity department.

Japanese: 言われたとおり、 手配をしただけです。
English: All I did was help out and do what I was asked.

Japanese: はあ‥‥なるほど。
English: Oh... I see.

Japanese: （はたして、そうだろうか‥‥）
English: (I wonder about that...)

Japanese: （このヒトが、状況も知らずに 仕事をするとは思えない‥‥）
English: (I wouldn't take her to be the type of person to do something without knowing the details.)

Japanese: あの、これなんですけど‥‥
English: Um, I'd like to ask you about this...

Japanese: ‥‥言ったはずです。 ムダなコトはキライだ、と。
English: I told you. I hate trifling matters.

Japanese: 私に関係ないものを 見せられても、困ります。
English: It's a waste of time to show me things that are of no relevance to me.

Japanese: （こんなに強く“キョゼツ” を感じるの、初めてだな‥‥）
English: (Wow... This is the first time I've been shut down this badly...)

Japanese: 荷星　三郎さん。 ‥‥私たち英都プロの俳優です。
English: That's Mr. Will Powers. He's an actor contracted with Global Studios.

Japanese: いい人です、とても。
English: He's a very nice man. A man with a good heart.

Japanese: ‥‥ただ、それだけですけど。
English: ...But that's all he is and ever will be.

Japanese: （ひゃあ‥‥ヒドいこと言うな）
English: (Urk... What a horrible thing to say...)

Japanese: ‥‥私たちの世界は、いい人には 向いていないのです。
