English: ...Society doesn't care about genuinely nice people, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （？　‥‥一瞬、キビしい 表情になったぞ‥‥）
English: (Huh? For a second, her face twisted in pain...)

Japanese: 何か、あったんでしょうか。 きりおさん‥‥。
English: I wonder if something happened to Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: 被害者のイサオさんとは、 お知り合いだったんですか？
English: Did you know the victim, Mr. Corrida?

Japanese: この世界はせまいですから。
English: Yes, I knew him.

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオさんのことは ‥‥存じ上げていました。
English: The world is such a small place, after all.

Japanese: ‥‥とのさまんさんと 張りあっていた、とか‥‥。
English: Did you know about his rivalry with the Nickel Samurai?

Japanese: ホントに‥‥彼と王都楼は 子供みたいでした。
English: Honestly, they were like children when it came to that.

Japanese: おたがい、つまらないことで 足を引っ張りあって‥‥。
English: Time and time again, those two competed with each other over the most uninteresting things.

Japanese: イジを張らなければ‥‥ 死なずにすんだのかも‥‥
English: If either one of them weren't so stubborn, then maybe no one would have needed to die.

Japanese: （‥‥この人は‥‥ 知ってるんじゃないか‥‥？）
English: (...I've got a hunch that this woman knows more than she's letting on...)

Japanese: （なぜ、藤見野　イサオが 殺害されたか‥‥その理由を！）
English: (She must know why Juan Corrida was killed!)

Japanese: 私からおねがいして マネージャーになりました。
English: I asked to become Mr. Engarde's manager.

Japanese: あつかいやすい、 スナオな俳優さんです。
English: He's a pleasure to manage with his nice disposition.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥オートロさん、 気が弱そうだもんな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... Mr. Engarde does seem like a rather weak-willed man; always doing as he's told...)

Japanese: 何かと言えば“マネージャーさん” ばっかりでしたね、オートロさん。
English: He's always saying, "my manager," right Mr. Nick?

Japanese: 自分のことを話すのは 大キライです。
English: I hate talking about myself.

Japanese: ‥‥ムダなこと、ですから。
English: It's a trifling matter, that's why.

Japanese: す、すみません‥‥。
English: S-Sorry...

Japanese: （冗談ヌキで、ホントに イヤみたいだな‥‥自分のコト）
English: (Take away that layer of sarcasm and she really does seem to hate herself...)

Japanese: ～殺害された理由は～
English: -- Motive for Murder --

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオさんがなぜ、 殺害されたのか‥‥？
English: Why was Juan Corrida murdered?

Japanese: あなたは何か、 知っているみたいですね。
English: If you ask me, I think you know the reason he was killed.

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: なぜ、かくすんですか。
English: Why are you hiding things?

Japanese: オートロさんのイノチが かかってるんですよ？
English: Don't you realize you're putting Mr. Engarde's life in danger by your actions?

Japanese: 知らないものは知りません。
English: Why do you ask questions for which I have no answer?

Japanese: 私、イサオさんとは、それほど 親しくありませんでしたから。
English: The truth is, I was not that close to Mr. Corrida.

Japanese: 親しくなかった‥‥？
English: You were not that close...?

Japanese: ええ。私、ニガテなんです。 他人と親しくなるのが‥‥
English: That's right. I've never been good at being intimate with another person.

Japanese: あなたが、他人とは親しくならない ‥‥本当にそうなのでしょうか。
English: You are not good at being intimate with another person? Somehow, I highly doubt that.

Japanese: 私、ヒトがニガテなんです。 なんだか、うっとうしくて。
English: I'm very bad at connecting with people. They're so depressing for the most part.

Japanese: はあ。
English: Um...

Japanese: アタマの悪いヒトは、 特にキライです。
English: But I especially dislike feeble-minded individuals.

Japanese: そうでしょうね。
English: Yes, well...

Japanese: だから、あなたとは 親しくなりたいと思いません。
English: That's why I don't think I could ever be intimate with you.

Japanese: （ぼくはうっとうしくて、さらに アタマが悪い、ってコトか）
English: (So... I guess she's saying I'm both depressing and stupid...?)

Japanese: しかし、あなたは被害者と 親しかったはずなんです。
English: But I am certain you were very close with the victim.

Japanese: そんなコトを言われても。 証拠はないでしょう‥‥？
English: Say that all you like, but you still don't have any proof.

Japanese: あなたとイサオさんは、 こういう関係だったのでは‥‥？
English: You and Mr. Corrida had an intimate relationship, did you not...?

Japanese: 三流雑誌のゴシップ記事。
English: A silly third-rate tabloid article.

Japanese: そんなもの、ふつう信じません。 ‥‥まともなニンゲンならば。
English: If you even had half your wits about you, you wouldn't believe such rubbish.

Japanese: みんな、けっこう信じてますよ。
English: Well, it seems quite a few people bought into this story.

Japanese: この世に、まともなニンゲンは おどろくほど少ないものです。
English: Hmph. As to be expected in a world filled with crooks and liars.
(...Note to self: Stay on her good side.)

Japanese: とにかく。私、キライなんです。 ‥‥ヒトと親しくなるのは。
English: In any case, I despise interpersonal relationships like that.

Japanese: そうですか。‥‥でも。
English: I see... However...

Japanese: どうしても、その必要が あったとしたら‥‥？
English: What if there was a need for you to get close to someone...?

Japanese: イサオさんと親しくなる必要 ‥‥そんなものが、あるのかしら？
English: Me? Need to get close to Mr. Corrida? As if there was ever such a need.

Japanese: あなたは、この人物のために、 イサオさんに近づいたのでは‥‥？
English: Didn't you get close to Mr. Corrida for this person's sake...?

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、 おわかりにならないのかしら。
English: ...Do you really still not understand?

Japanese: な、何がですか‥‥？
English: Wh-What?

Japanese: 私はキホン的に、だれかのために 動くことはありません。
English: It's not in my nature to enjoy doing things for others.

Japanese: で、でもあなたは、 マネージャーという‥‥
English: B-But... You're a manager...

Japanese: ‥‥仕事ですから。
English: It's called a job.

Japanese: 王都楼は、私が担当している タレント。‥‥それだけのコト。
English: I am in charge of Mr. Engarde the star, not Matt Engarde the man.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥
English: O-Oh.

Japanese: そしてあなたは、 私の担当ですらありません。
English: And you are not my client.

Japanese: （早いハナシ、 “帰れ”ってか‥‥）
English: (Is this my subtle hint to "go home"...?)

Japanese: ‥‥それでも。
English: But...

Japanese: あなたにはどうしても、被害者と 親しくなる必要があったんです。
English: I know you must have had a need to get close to the victim.

Japanese: 天野　由利恵さん。 ‥‥あなたの先輩です。
English: Celeste Inpax, your mentor.

Japanese: なぜあなたが‥‥ 由利恵さんのことを！
English: Why do you know about Celeste!?

Japanese: 由利恵さん‥‥ 自殺されたそうですね。
English: Ms. Inpax... She committed suicide, didn't she?

Japanese: しかも、その理由は ‥‥不明だそうですね。
English: But it looks like no one knows why.

Japanese: 彼女は死の直前、藤見野　イサオの マネージャーをしていた。
English: Right before her death, she was Juan Corrida's manager.

Japanese: あなたは‥‥彼女の自殺を調べる ために、彼に近づいたのでは？
English: So, I believe you got close to Mr. Corrida so you could find out more about her suicide.

Japanese: ‥‥た、たいした想像力ね。
English: ...Y-You have a great imagination.

Japanese: 三流の、クズ雑誌のゴミ記者にでも なったらいかがかしら！
English: You may have a future yet as a slimy muckraker for a putrid third-rate tabloid!

Japanese: き‥‥霧緒さん？
English: M-Ms. Andrews...?

Japanese: ゆ、由利恵さんの死に、ナゾなんて ありません。
English: Th-There was no mystery surrounding her death. None.

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオに近づいても、 イミなんてないわ！
English: It would be pointless for me to force myself into a relationship for nothing!

Japanese: （‥‥はたして、 そうだったんだろうか‥‥？）
English: (Is that really true...? Was there really no mystery at all?)

Japanese: 由利恵さんの自殺に‥‥あなたは ナットクできたハズがない！
English: I don't believe you were completely at ease with the way her suicide was resolved!

Japanese: ‥‥いいかげんにしてください。
English: Get a hold of yourself.
Huh?

Japanese: 自殺のやりかたが知りたいなら、 警察にでもお聞きなさいな！
English: If you want advice on doing yourself in, I suggest asking someone, like the police.

Japanese: （早いハナシ、ぼくに “死ね”ってか‥‥）
English: (Which translates to... "Go take a long walk off a short pier"...?)

Japanese: ‥‥由利恵さんの遺書、 発見されなかったそうですね。
English: ...Ms. Inpax's suicide note was never found, was it?

Japanese: 警察は、だれかが遺書をかくした、 と考えていたみたいです。
English: It looks like the police were under the suspicion that someone had hidden it.

Japanese: たとえば‥‥死体を発見した、 藤見野　イサオさんが。
English: Like maybe the person who discovered her body, Mr. Corrida.

Japanese: ふ‥‥藤見野　イサオが‥‥
English: J... Juan?

Japanese: そして、霧緒さん！ あなたもそう考えたんです！
English: And, Ms. Andrews, I believe you thought the same thing!

Japanese: だから、彼に近づいた！
English: That is why you became intimate with Mr. Corrida!

Japanese: お、おとなしく聞いていれば‥‥ 勝手なコトばっかり！
English: I-I've sat by quietly and listened to your insulting ramblings long enough!

Japanese: た、たしかに由利恵さんは 私の先輩でした。
English: It's true that Celeste was my mentor, however, allow me to say this again:

Japanese: でも、だからと言って‥‥ 私にはカンケイない！
English: It had nothing to do with me!

Japanese: “遺書が見つからない”？ わ、私の知ったことじゃないわ！
English: I didn't even know that her suicide note was never found!

Japanese: 私は‥‥他人のコトなんて、 どうだっていい人間なんです！
English: I'm a person who doesn't care about what goes on in the lives of others!

Japanese: （たしかに、そうみたいだ。 しかし‥‥）
English: (That's the impression you like to give, however...)

Japanese: 本当に、そうなのでしょうか。
English: I don't think that's who you really are.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと‥‥？
English: What?

Japanese: ‥‥証拠があるんです。
English: I have evidence that says otherwise.

Japanese: 天野　由利恵さんが、あなたに とって特別だったという証拠が‥‥
English: This is proof that Celeste Inpax was someone very special to you...

Japanese: ‥‥どうですか？　霧緒さん。
English: Well, how about it, Ms. Andrews?

Japanese: あなどれないわね、 ベンゴシという連中も。
English: I'm sure even those among your lawyer colleagues find it hard to hate you.

Japanese: あやうく、自分を見失う ところでした。
English: They probably pity you. After all, you are dangerously close to being a failure.

Japanese: “あやうく”‥‥？
English: "Pity"...?

Japanese: おかげさまで、もう 取りもどしましたけど。
English: Although, thanks to your little spectacle, I feel much better about myself.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥もしかして、 まだ証拠が足りないのか‥‥？）
English: (*sigh* This evidence isn't strong enough to give up the truth.)

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろ、あなたにも わかったのではないかしら？
English: ...By now, even someone like you should understand.

Japanese: ‥‥霧緒さん‥‥あなたにも、 経験があるそうですね。
English: Ms. Andrews... You... You went through it too, didn't you?

Japanese: なんの経験‥‥かしら？
English: Went through what?

Japanese: “自殺”ですよ、もちろん。
English: A suicide.

Japanese: 霧緒さん。あなたはとても しっかりしているように見える。
English: Ms. Andrews, you look and act like a very strong woman who has it all together.

Japanese: だれの助けもいらない。 ひとりで生きているように見える。
English: You don't ask for anyone's help and you live by yourself.

Japanese: そ、そうよ。私はいつだって、 ひとりで生きてきた‥‥
English: Y-Yes. I've been very independent ever since I can remember...

Japanese: しかし、それはウソだった。
English: However, that is all just a lie. A façade.

Japanese: あなたはいつも、依存できる 人間を探しているそうですね。
English: You've always searched out people on whom you can depend on.

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それは‥‥ッ！
English: Th-That's...!

Japanese: あなたは、由利恵さんに すべてをゆだねていた。
English: You were dependent on Ms. Inpax, weren't you?

Japanese: だから‥‥
English: Which is why...

Japanese: 彼女がいなくなったとき、 あなたはすべてを失った。
English: When she passed away, you lost everything you had.

Japanese: ‥‥やめて‥‥ッ！
English: S-Stop!

Japanese: 由利恵さんは突然、 いなくなってしまった‥‥
English: When Celeste passed away so suddenly like that...

Japanese: 一度は死のうとしたわ。 ‥‥でも‥‥
English: I died a death of my own. But...

Japanese: どうしても気になったの。 ‥‥消えた、遺書が‥‥
English: No matter how hard I tried, I couldn't stop thinking about what had become of her note.

Japanese: 遺書は、藤見野　イサオが かくしたらしい‥‥
English: You must have heard about the police report...

Japanese: その情報を当然、あなたは 知っていたはずです。
English: The one that said the police suspected Mr. Corrida of hiding Ms. Inpax's note...

Japanese: あなたは、それを取りもどすため、 彼に近づいたんですね？
English: You heard about it, and thought to recover it from him by getting close, am I right?

Japanese: ‥‥そうなると、 ハナシは変わってくる。
English: If that's the case, then everything changes.

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どういうこと‥‥？
English: Wh-What do you mean?

Japanese: そもそもぼくたちは、 何について話していたのか？
English: What topic did we start this conversation on again?

Japanese: “被害者がなぜ殺害されたか” ‥‥でしょう？
English: It was "why was the victim killed"...

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。
English: Exactly.

Japanese: どうやら、霧緒さん。
English: Somehow, Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: その理由を持っているのは‥‥ あなた自身じゃないんですか？
English: It seems that you have become the one most likely to want Mr. Corrida dead...

Japanese: あなたにとって、由利恵さんは すべてだった。
English: Ms. Inpax was everything to you.

Japanese: ‥‥その彼女が死んだ。
English: ...And then she died.

Japanese: 理由を知るためならば、あなたは どんなことでもしたでしょう。
English: And you would do anything to find out why she killed herself.

Japanese: ‥‥殺人でも、ね。
English: ...Even commit murder.

Japanese: ‥‥さつじん‥‥！
English: Murder...!?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 王都楼　真悟の控え室
English: March 21 Gatewater Hotel Engarde's Hotel Room

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。 霧緒さん、いませんね。
English: ...Hmm, looks like Ms. Andrews isn't here.

Japanese: まいったな。彼女には まだ、聞きたいことがあったのに。
English: That's not good. I still have a few questions I want to ask her...

Japanese: サイコ・ロックも‥‥ ありましたしね。
English: And she has that Psyche-Lock on her heart, right?

Japanese: ‥‥しかたないな。 また、あとで来てみようか。
English: ...Well, we don't have much of a choice. I guess we'll have to come back later.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 王都楼　真悟の控え室
English: March 21 Gatewater Hotel Engarde's Hotel Room

Japanese: ‥‥あ。霧緒さんが いらっしゃいますよ。
English: ...Oh! Ms. Andrews is here.

Japanese: どなたかと、おはなし されているようですけど‥‥
English: But it looks like she's talking with someone...

Japanese: （あれは‥‥狩魔　冥！）
English: (That's... Franziska von Karma!)

Japanese: かるま検事さん‥‥？
English: Ms. von Karma...?

Japanese: こんなところで 何をしているッ！
English: What are you doing here!?

Japanese: い、いや、その。 ぼく、弁護士だから‥‥
English: Um, well, you see... I'm his lawyer, so...

Japanese: 私のあとをつけるなんて、 いいドキョウね‥‥
English: You've got some nerve, following me around...

Japanese: あとをつける‥‥？
English: Following you...?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥かるま検事！ あなたのほうではないですかっ！
English: Th-That's you, Ms. von Karma! You're the one doing the following!

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥
English: Pearls...

Japanese: あの、おヒゲの刑事さんの あとをつけまわして‥‥！
English: You're always following after that Mr. Detective with the little beard!

Japanese: ‥‥私が？　ヒゲのあとを‥‥？ バカバカしい！
English: ...Me? Following after Scruffy...? Don't make me laugh.

Japanese: おじょうちゃん。 ‥‥いいものを見せてあげるわ。
English: I'll show you something interesting... little girl.

Japanese: ‥ピピッ‥‥ピピッ‥‥ピピッ‥
English: ...*beep* *beep*... ...*beep* *beep*... ...*beep* *beep*...

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥なんですか？
English: Wh-What is that?

Japanese: 電波受信機よ。
English: An electromagnetic receiver.

Japanese: あの刑事のカラダには、 発信器がとりつけてあるの。
English: I planted a tracking device on that detective.

Japanese: これがあれば‥‥あの男の 動きは、カンペキにわかるわ。
English: And with this, I know that fool's every move.

Japanese: （この音は、電波受信機の ものだったのか‥‥）
English: (So that noise we heard was this receiver...)

Japanese: （キノドクなイトノコ刑事‥‥）
English: (I feel really sorry for poor Detective Gumshoe now...)

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Now then...

Japanese: 思わぬところで 時間をムダにしたわ。
English: Let's stop wasting time.

Japanese: ‥‥華宮　霧緒ッ！
English: Adrian Andrews!

Japanese: は、はい‥‥
English: Y-Yes...?

Japanese: さっきの話‥‥ 真剣に考えておくことね！
English: Think hard about what we just discussed. Understood!?

Japanese: え、ええ‥‥
English: A-Alright...

Japanese: （‥‥何を話していたんだ？ この２人‥‥）
English: (What were those two talking about...?)

Japanese: きりおさん‥‥、なんか ボンヤリしていますね。
English: Ms. Andrews... She seems a little dazed, doesn't she?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 藤見野　イサオの控え室
English: March 21 Gatewater Hotel Corrida's Hotel Room

Japanese: なるほどくん。ここは‥‥
English: Mr. Nick... Where are we...?

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオの控え室だよ。
English: We're in Mr. Juan Corrida's room, Pearls.

Japanese: ふじみのさん‥‥
English: Mr. Corrida...?

Japanese: 被害者だ。 ‥‥つまり、殺人現場だね。
English: The victim... Which makes this the crime scene too.

Japanese: おッ！　アンタ！
English: Oh! It's you!

Japanese: どッスか！　連絡あったッスか？ ‥‥ユーカイ犯。
English: So!? What's happened!? The kidnapper! Has he contacted you again!?

Japanese: ‥‥いえ。たぶん、 無罪判決を勝ちとるまでは‥‥
English: Not yet. He probably won't until we win Mr. Engarde's acquittal.

Japanese: ふうううん‥‥ ツラいッスな、アンタも。
English: Umm... You doing OK, pal? Hanging in there?

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥ ブジでいてくれよ‥‥）
English: (I just want Maya to be alright...)

Japanese: 時間がないッスが、できるかぎり 協力、するッス。
English: We don't have a lot of time left, but I'm going to help you as much as I can, pal.

Japanese: いいんですか？ 現場を調べても‥‥。
English: Can you do that? Even if we want to look around the crime scene...?

Japanese: トクベツッス。
English: Just this once... "Special circumstances" right, pal?

Japanese: 情報もタレ流すッス。 ‥‥ゼッタイ、ヒミツッスよ。
English: I'll even tell you everything I know. But you've gotta keep quiet.

Japanese: 自分のイノチにかかわるッス。
English: It's my neck on the line here.

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you.

Japanese: あ。それからコレ。 ‥‥ホテルの見取り図ッス。
English: Oh, that's right. I got you guys a map of the hotel, pal.

Japanese: ハイ。おじょうちゃんに あげるッス。
English: Here you go, little missy.

Japanese: わあ。ホントですか！ うれしいです‥‥。
English: Wow, you're giving it to me!? Thank you!

Japanese: ハッハッ。‥‥バカみたいに 広いホテルッスからねー。
English: Ha ha. Wouldn't want you to get lost in a hotel too big for its own good.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 もらっちゃいました！
English: Mr. Nick! I got a map!

Japanese: よかったね、春美ちゃん。
English: That's great, Pearls!

Japanese: ‥‥でもわたくし、 漢字が読めないのでした‥‥。
English: Um... But Mr. Nick, I can't read what it says...

Japanese: 《ホテルの見取り図》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Hotel Guidemap added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥ピピッ‥‥ピピッ‥‥ピピッ‥
English: ...*beep* *beep*... ...*beep* *beep*... ...*beep* *beep*...

Japanese: な、なんですか？　なるほどくん。 ‥‥この妙な音は‥‥
English: Wh-What's that beeping noise, Mr. Nick...?

Japanese: （なんか‥‥どこかで 聞いたおぼえがあるぞ‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I've heard this sound somewhere before...)

Japanese: 狩魔検事ッス‥‥
English: It's Ms. von Karma...
Huh?

Japanese: この音が聞こえると、なぜか 狩魔検事がやって来るッス！
English: Whenever I hear that sound, for some reason, she pops out of nowhere and whips me!

Japanese: （あ‥‥。そういえば、 前にもそんなことがあったっけ）
English: (Come to think of it, that did happen the last time...)

Japanese: じじじ、自分はこれで シツレイするッス！
English: S-Sorry! I've gotta make myself scarce! Later, pal!

Japanese: ぎゃはァ！
English: Eowww!

Japanese: ついに本性を見せたな ‥‥成歩堂　龍一ッ‥‥！
English: At last, you reveal your true nature... Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: （ううう‥‥コワくて コトバが出てこないぞ‥‥）
English: (*gulp* Would it be too much to ask for you to be nice to me for a change...?)

Japanese: キサマというヤツは‥‥
English: So you're the type to steal information from pitifully hopeless detectives!

Japanese: 刑事から情報を盗むとは！ なんという、ヒキョウなッ！
English: That's very dishonorable of you!

Japanese: （‥‥いててて‥‥）
English: (Owww!)

Japanese: コラッ！　逃げるな ヒゲコートッ！
English: Hey! Don't you dare run away, Scruffy McTrenchcoat!

Japanese: はッ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: この国の刑事のモラルが ここまで低いとは思わなかった‥‥
English: I didn't think the detectives of this country could be this pitiful...

Japanese: むはあァァッ！
English: Uwaah!

Japanese: 刑事！　ちょっと こっちへ来なさいッ！
English: Detective! Come over here for a second!

Japanese: ぎゃああああああああああああああ あああああああああああああッス！
English: Yeoooooooooooooooooooo oooooooooooooooooooooo ooooooooooooooooooooow!

Japanese: ‥‥ほほお。
English: ...Hmph.

Japanese: このスキに 逃げなかったとは、感心ね‥‥
English: I feel better knowing at least you were man enough to face your punishment.

Japanese: （あ‥‥足がすくんで 動けない‥‥）
English: (He was so scared, he just froze up on the spot...)

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一！
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: 私の‥‥検事としての経歴は、 ドロにまみれてしまった‥‥
English: You... You have soiled my perfect prosecution record.

Japanese: キサマのせいで！ ‥‥ユルせない‥‥
English: I'll never forget that.

Japanese: 今度こそ‥‥キサマに勝つ！
English: This time... Victory is mine!

Japanese: ぼくに‥‥“勝つ”。 そればっかりだな、きみは‥‥
English: Victory is yours? Is that all this means to you...?

Japanese: な、なんですって‥‥？
English: Wh-What...!?
H-Hmph!

Japanese: 来なさい、ヒゲ！ ‥‥捜査会議が始まるわ！
English: Come, Scruff-face! The investigation briefing is about to begin.

Japanese: はは‥‥ハッ！
English: Y-Yes, sir!

Japanese: 狩魔家のホコリにかけて‥‥ 決着をつけてやるわ！
English: This isn't over yet... I swear on my family's honor!

Japanese: （‥‥いてて。狩魔検事のヤツ、 何か投げつけていったぞ‥‥）
English: (Ow! Now what did she throw at me...!?)

Japanese: （なんだ？　これ‥‥）
English: (What is this...?)

Japanese: ‥‥というコトで、自分は 署のほうへもどるッス。
English: ...Well, I guess this means I've gotta get back to the precinct now, pal.

Japanese: 自分に用ができたら、 警察署に来るッス。
English: If you ever need me, come down to the Criminal Affairs Department, alright?

Japanese: くれぐれも、狩魔検事に 見つからないように‥‥
English: And if you can, try not to let Ms. von Karma see you...

Japanese: 冷蔵庫の中は‥‥ いろいろ入ってるな。
English: There are all sorts of things in this refrigerator...

Japanese: ‥‥ニンジンジュースと‥‥ トマトジュースの空きビンです。
English: The carrot juice bottle and the tomato juice bottle are both empty...

Japanese: 空きビン‥‥？ （捨てるのがメンドウだったのか）
English: Empty...? (It's too much of a hassle to throw them away, I guess.)

Japanese: 野菜ジュースばっかりだね。 カラダに気をつかってたのかな。
English: They're all vegetable juices. I guess he must've been a real health nut.

Japanese: ‥‥あ。キムチとケチャップと イチゴジャムもありました。
English: ...Oh! There's a beet, some ketchup, and a bottle of strawberry jam too!

Japanese: ‥‥ただ赤い色が好きな だけかもしれないなあ。
English: Maybe red was his favorite color...?

Japanese: クマさんが ひしめいています！
English: Wow, there are a lot of bears!

Japanese: （目覚まし時計やら置物やら ぬいぐるみやらプラモデルやら）
English: (Alarm clock ones, collector's editions, stuffed teddies, plastic models...)

Japanese: （圧倒的な光景だな‥‥）
English: (It's pretty overwhelming... Is there a kind of bear he doesn't have...?)

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ゴミ箱の中にも つっこまれてますけど‥‥。
English: There's even a few in the trash can...

Japanese: うーん‥‥本人は、あまり クマ好きじゃないのかも。
English: Yeah... I get the feeling maybe the guy didn't really like bears.

Japanese: クマさん、カワイソウです‥‥
English: Poor teddies...

Japanese: いやーホント、クマったもんだ。 ‥‥なーんて。
English: It's hard to "bear" with all these problems... Growl!

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃらひゃら。あひゃ！ ひゃひゃひゃらあひゃあひゃ。ひゃ
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: （こないだの事件‥‥ある種、 トラウマになっちまったな）
English: (I don't think I want to "bear" with the trauma the last case caused me...)
What's wrong, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: もう！ こんなにちらかして‥‥
English: Hmph! It's so messy here!

Japanese: （ホント、キレイ好きだな 春美ちゃんは‥‥）
English: (Pearls really likes things neat and tidy, I take it...)

Japanese: それにしても、見たことのない キカイがいっぱいありますね。
English: There are a lot of electronic things in here that I've never seen before.

Japanese: ね。なるほどくん。 教えてください！
English: Hey, Mr. Nick! Tell me what they are, pretty please?

Japanese: そこにあるのがウデ時計だよ。 手首につけるんだ。
English: OK. That over there is a "watch". You wear it on your wrist.

Japanese: ‥‥知ってます。
English: I know what a watch is...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった。相手は 真宵ちゃんじゃないんだった）
English: (Oops. For a second there, I forgot I was talking with Pearls, not Maya...)

Japanese: トランクだ。いろんなものが ギュウと詰めこまれている。
English: It's a suitcase. There are so many things in it that I bet it barely closes.

Japanese: 着替えにドライヤー、 ヒゲそりに電卓、か‥‥。
English: There's his clothes, a dryer, an electric shaver, a calculator...

Japanese: 旅行に関係ないものが まじっていますね。
English: Do all stars pack too much stuff like Mr. Corrida?

Japanese: これがウワサの “ベッド”というものですか‥‥。
English: So that's a bed, right?

Japanese: う、うん。ウワサのね。
English: Yup. It's big, but it's a bed.

Japanese: きゃあ！　なるほどくん！ ふかふかですッ！
English: Aaah! Mr. Nick! It's sooooo soft!

Japanese: （大事件みたいだ‥‥）
English: (Big beds must be a rarity for her...)

Japanese: イサオさん、 食事したみたいだね。
English: It looks like Mr. Corrida had dinner last night.

Japanese: このビン‥‥ トマトジュースです。
English: This bottle... It's tomato juice.

Japanese: （授賞式でゴチソウが たくさん並んでいたけど‥‥）
English: (We had a lot of food at the award show last night...)

Japanese: （スターって、 ハラがへるのかなあ）
English: (But I wonder if the stars had gone onstage after only eating a meager meal like this...)

Japanese: 化粧品のビンが 散乱している。
English: Bottles of cosmetics are scattered all over the floor.

Japanese: ‥‥ここで、イサオさんは 犯人と争ったのだろう。
English: This is probably where Mr. Corrida fought his assailant.

Japanese: この、ガラスのカケラは なんでしょう‥‥？
English: What's this glass fragment from...?

Japanese: 花ビン‥‥かな？ 名もない花も落ちてるし。
English: A flower vase maybe? There are flowers on the floor, but I don't know what they are.

Japanese: なるほどくんが 知らないだけです。
English: You don't know much about flowers, do you Mr. Nick?

Japanese: これは‥‥ギターケース、だな。 カラッポだけど。
English: This is... a guitar case, I guess. A little beat up, but still useable.

Japanese: ヘンですね。どこにも ありませんよ‥‥ギター。
English: That's strange. The guitar is not here...

Japanese: 持ってくるのを 忘れたのかな。
English: Maybe he forgot to bring it to the show?

Japanese: たしか、真宵さまが おっしゃっていましたよね。
English: But Mystic Maya...

Japanese: 忍者ナンジャは、まっかなギターが トレード・マークだ、と‥‥。
English: She said the bright red guitar was the Jammin' Ninja's signature item...

Japanese: （‥‥そういえば‥‥）
English: (That's true...)

Japanese: ん？
English: Huh?

Japanese: このギターケース、ぬれてるぞ。 フタのところだけ‥‥。
English: This guitar case is wet. But it's only wet on top of the lid...

Japanese: お水‥‥ですね、これ。 ケースの中はぬれていません。
English: Yeah... There's no water inside the case... This is water, isn't it...?

Japanese: 証拠品《ギターケース》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Guitar Case added to the Court Record.

Japanese: キレイなグラスだな。 中身は‥‥トマトジュースか。
English: It's a beautiful wine glass, and there's... tomato juice in it.

Japanese: わたくし、ニガテです ‥‥トマトジュース。
English: Ewww. Tomato juice. I don't really like it much.

Japanese: テーブルにボトルがあるね。 きっとあれだよ。
English: There's a bottle of it on the table over there. That's probably where this came from.

Japanese: でも‥‥ ちょっとヘンですね。
English: But doesn't it seem weird?

Japanese: ‥‥何が？
English: ...What seems weird?

Japanese: だって、他のものはみんな、 ちらかっています。
English: I mean, everything else is scattered all over the floor.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥花ビンは割れてるし 化粧品のビンも床に落ちている）
English: (She's right. The flower vase was broken and the make-up is strewn everywhere.)

Japanese: なぜこのグラスだけ、 何ごともなかったのでしょう‥‥？
English: Why is this glass the only thing that's still alright...?

Japanese: 証拠品《グラス》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Wine Glass added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なるほどくん。このラクガキは どういう‥‥？
English: Mr. Nick, what's this piece of paper...?

Japanese: それ、サインだよ。
English: It's called an "autograph".

Japanese: さいん‥‥ですか？
English: Auto-graff?

Japanese: イサオさんの名前が 書いてあるんだ。
English: It's got Mr. Corrida's name written on it, so it's his autograph.

Japanese: ゼンゼン読めませんね。
English: I can't read it at all.

Japanese: 正直に言って、こんなヘタな字、 見たことがありません。
English: To be honest, I've never seen writing that looks like this.

Japanese: いやいや。それはワザとなの。
English: Ah, it's a special way of writing called "cursive".

Japanese: ホラ、“カオルちゃんへ”って 書いてあるでしょ？
English: Look here. See how it says, "To my dearest Wendy" in more normal letters here?

Japanese: こ、こんな読めない字をヒトサマに あげるなんて‥‥非常識です！
English: Th-This sloppy, unreadable writing! It's crazy and cruel to give this to someone!

Japanese: （‥‥ちょっと待てよ‥‥）
English: (Hold on...)

Japanese: ‥‥？ どうかしましたか、なるほどくん？
‥‥カオル‥‥って、どこかで 聞いたような名前だな、って‥‥。
English: Wendy... I've heard that name somewhere before...

Japanese: イサオさんの《サイン色紙》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Mr. Corrida's Autograph added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 被害者の‥‥イサオさんの死因は、 わかったんですか？
English: Do you know what was the cause of death?

Japanese: 正式な解剖記録はまだッス。 でも‥‥
English: Well, technically, the final autopsy report isn't out yet, but...

Japanese: 現場をひとめ見れば、 アキラカだと思うッス。
English: one look at the scene of the crime should tell you, pal.

Japanese: 現場を‥‥？
English: The scene of the crime...?

Japanese: これが、その写真ッス。
English: Yeah. Here's a picture.

Japanese: ムネに‥‥ナイフが 刺さってますね。
English: There's a knife in his chest.

Japanese: おそらくそれが、凶器ッス。
English: Yeah, pal, that's the murder weapon.

Japanese: （刺殺されたわけ‥‥か）
English: (So he was stabbed to death...)

Japanese: 今、鑑識で、ナイフの指紋を 調べているッス。
English: They're looking at the fingerprints down at the lab right now.

Japanese: ‥‥ナイフに指紋が 残っていたんですか？
English: There were fingerprints on the knife?

Japanese: そッス。
English: Yup.

Japanese: ほぼまちがいなく、指紋は 王都楼のものッスよ。
English: And it looks like they're pretty sure they're Mr. Engarde's prints, pal.
(That's bad... Real bad...)

Japanese: 証拠品《現場写真》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Crime Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: どうしてオートロさんが 逮捕されたんですか？
English: Why was Mr. Engarde arrested?

Japanese: モチロン、証拠があったッス。
English: Because we had evidence on him.

Japanese: しょうこ‥‥
English: Evidence...?

Japanese: 被害者の藤見野　イサオは、 かなり抵抗したみたいッス。
English: It looks like the victim, Juan Corrida, really put up a big fight.

Japanese: ‥‥現場を見れば、 アキラカですね。
English: Yeah, one look at the crime scene and you can tell.

Japanese: 争ったあとが、なまなましく 残っていますから。
English: There's signs of a struggle everywhere.

Japanese: ま、そッスね。 そのとき、ボタンがちぎれて‥‥
English: Well, yeah. During the fight, his button came off...

Japanese: （オートロさんが 言ってたっけ‥‥）
English: (Mr. Engarde said something about a button...)

Japanese: （衣装のハカマに、忍者ナンジャの ボタンが挟まっていた、って）
English: (Something like, one of the Jammin' Ninja's buttons got caught in his "hakama"...)

Japanese: ‥‥それだけじゃないッス。
English: But that's not all...

Japanese: なんですって！
English: What!?

Japanese: 目撃者がいたッス。
English: There was a witness, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥目撃者！ いったい、ダレですか！
English: A witness!? Wh-Who is it!?

Japanese: ‥‥あのオバチャンッス。
English: That lady... Ms. Oldbag.
(Please... Anyone but her!)

Japanese: 証拠、証言ともバッチリッス。 ただ‥‥。
English: The prosecution has plenty of evidence to make a solid case...

Japanese: 現場にちょっと、フシギな点が あるのも、たしかッス。
English: Not to mention there's something at the crime scene that was a little off.

Japanese: フシギな点‥‥って、 なんですか？
English: Something that was a little off...? As in...?

Japanese: 自分で調べるッス！
English: As in, that's for you to figure out, pal!

Japanese: 調べてみましょう！ なるほどくん！
English: Alright, let's try to figure it out, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: このギターケースですけど‥‥。
English: What can you tell me about this guitar case?

Japanese: ああ、それ。
English: Oh, that.

Japanese: 聞いた話ッスが、忍者ナンジャの トレードマークらしいッスね。
English: This is just what I heard, pal, but that's the Jammin' Ninja's signature item.

Japanese: ギターケースがですか？
English: The guitar case?

Japanese: そんなワケないッス。 中身のギターッス。もちろん。
English: No, not that! What do you take me as? I mean the guitar inside, of course!

Japanese: でも‥‥どこにもありませんよ、 ギターなんて。
English: But... The guitar is missing.

Japanese: ワレワレも今、探してるッス。
English: Yeah, we looked for it too.

Japanese: 授賞式にトレードマークを 忘れるなんて、不自然ッスから。
English: It's not normal for a person to forget to bring their most famous item to an award show.

Japanese: （まっかなギター‥‥か。 カンケイあるのかな、事件に）
English: (It's starting to sound like that red guitar is related to this case after all...)

Japanese: このグラスですけど‥‥
English: So about this wine glass...

Japanese: おッ。さすが。気づいたッスか。
English: Ah, so you noticed it, pal.

Japanese: 現場はヒドく乱れているのに、 そのグラスだけ、なんともない。
English: The whole crime scene was a mess, but this glass was the only thing that was untouched.

Japanese: あ。やっぱりイトノコさんも‥‥？
English: You noticed that too, Detective Gumshoe...?

Japanese: いや、その。狩魔検事が先に 気づいちゃったッス。
English: No, actually Ms. von Karma noticed it first.

Japanese: ぼくも、春美ちゃんが先に ‥‥って、
English: Yeah... Pearls noticed it before me too... Hey, wait a minute.

Japanese: 狩魔検事、来てるんですか？ ‥‥ここに。
English: So... does that mean Ms. von Karma's coming here...?

Japanese: 来てるッス。‥‥もう、 タイヘンなコトになってるッス。
English: Yeah, she's coming. Man, you're going to be in so much trouble, pal.

Japanese: アンタは、ゼッタイ 会わないほうがいいと思うッスよ。
English: I think it'd be best if you didn't bump into her here.

Japanese: ‥‥見かけたら、走って逃げます。
English: You can bet the instant I see her, I'll be running the 1,000 meter dash.

Japanese: ‥‥それッスか。ユーカイ犯 からのプレゼント。
English: That's your "present" from the kidnapper, huh?

Japanese: 通話中に、電波が発信される ッスから‥‥、
English: While you're talking, it sends and receives radio waves...

Japanese: それをたどっていけば、 あるいは‥‥。
English: If we could trace that...

Japanese: ハンニンの場所が わかるのですね！
English: Yeah! We could use it to find out where the bad guy is!

Japanese: （デンパ、か‥‥）
English: (It uses radio waves, huh?)

Japanese: わかったッス！
English: I got it!

Japanese: 電波探知機、手配しとくッス。 あとで貸してあげるッス！
English: I'll go find a radio scanner, and let you borrow it, pal!

Japanese: こう言っちゃあ なんスけど‥‥
English: You look at this photo and you can't help it.

Japanese: “まさにサツジンゲンバ” ‥‥って感じッスねえ。
English: It makes you want to say, "I can't believe this is the scene of a murder."

Japanese: （争ったあとに、 突き立ったナイフ‥‥）
English: (Stabbed with a knife after a struggle...)

Japanese: （この中に、きっと何か、 手がかりが眠っているはずだ）
English: (A clue is sleeping in this photo somewhere... I know it.)

Japanese: ほお‥‥その記事、 アンタも気にしてるッスか‥‥
English: Oh... So you saw that article too...

Japanese: ‥‥え。ベツにそういう つもりでは‥‥
English: Yeah. But I'm not really interested in that sort of thing...

Japanese: じつは、ワレワレも 調査してるッス。‥‥その密会。
English: Actually, we've been looking into that too, pal... Those secret meetings, I mean.

Japanese: （やれやれ。ケイサツも ケイハクになったもんだ‥‥）
English: (What is this world coming to when even the cops are amateur gossip collectors...?)

Japanese: チガうッス！
English: Hey, why are you looking at me like that, pal!? Whatever you're thinking, you're wrong!

Japanese: ‥‥ま。調査結果が出たら、 聞かせてあげるッス。
English: ...Um, look, when we find out more from our investigation, I'll let you know, OK?

Japanese: ちょっぴり ヤセた気がするッス。
English: I look good! I think I've lost some weight, pal!

Japanese: 気のせいですよ。
English: It's only wishful thinking.

Japanese: 気のせいッスか。
English: Wishful thinking, huh?

Japanese: やっぱり ヤセた気がするッス。
English: In that case, I wishfully think I've lost some weight!

Japanese: （だから、気のせいだって！）
English: (I wishfully think he might face the facts one day...)

Japanese: 最近、ソーメンしか 食べてないッスからねえ‥‥。
English: Lately, I've been on the instant noodles only diet, pal!

Japanese: ‥‥いッスか、おじょうちゃん。
English: Hey, little missy. Listen good, OK?

Japanese: はい。なんでしょうか。
English: OK. What is it?

Japanese: ムチにだけは、 キョーミを持っちゃダメッス。
English: Don't ever pick up a whip, OK? Don't even think about it.

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: Huh!?

Japanese: それから、どんなに アタマにくるコトがあっても、
English: If you ever find yourself thinking you want one,

Japanese: ビンタでガマンするッス。 ‥‥ビンタで。
English: try to make due with a good slap to the face. Got that? Slap the person in the face.

Japanese: オジさんとヤクソクッス。
English: Promise me, OK?

Japanese: （そうとうイタめつけられてる みたいだな‥‥）
English: (Personal experience tells me Gumshoe should be careful what he wishes for...)

Japanese: すまねッス。
English: I'm sorry.

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんですか？
English: ...For what?

Japanese: ホントは自分も、彼女の捜査を してあげたいッス。
English: I really want to help you look for her, pal.

Japanese: けいじさん‥‥
English: Mr. Detective...

Japanese: 何か状況が変わったら、 すぐ教えるッス。
English: If anything happens, let me know, OK?

Japanese: できるかぎりのコトは するッス！
English: I want to help in any way I can!

Japanese: 今の彼女は、都会のジャングルに はなたれた野獣ッス。
English: She's like a wild beast let loose in the asphalt jungle.

Japanese: やじゅう‥‥
English: A beast...?

Japanese: ハラをすかせたトラより キケンッス。猛りくるってるッス。
English: She's more dangerous than a starving tiger. She's a fierce one, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥やはり、２つの敗訴が ヒビいているのでしょうか‥‥？
English: Is she mad because she lost two cases...?

Japanese: ヒビきわたってるッス！ 検事局は今や、崩壊寸前ッス！
English: Mad? She's more than mad; she's furious! The precinct is barely holding on!

Japanese: 狩魔検事は、“半殺し”のイミを 正確に知っているッス。
English: Thanks to her, now I know what being "half-dead" really means.

Japanese: ‥‥キッチリ半分、 死んだキモチにさせるッス。
English: She really makes you feel like you're almost a shambling zombie.

Japanese: （非常口だけは カクニンしておこう‥‥）
English: (All I know is when I'm around her, I always check for the emergency exits.)

Japanese: 宇宙人かと思ったッス。
English: I thought she was an alien from outer space.

Japanese: ああ。ミョーなヘルメット、 かぶってますからねえ。
English: Yeah, probably because she's wearing that strange helmet.

Japanese: ヘルメット取っても 宇宙人かと思ったッス。
English: Nah, even with her helmet off, I thought she was an alien, pal.

Japanese: それは言わないほうが いいと思いますよ。
English: I think that's probably best kept to yourself, if you know what I mean...

Japanese: できれば見たくないカオ、 第３位ッス。
English: If I had a choice, she would be number three on my list of people I don't want to see.

Japanese: ‥‥どうせ、第１位は ぼく、なんでしょう？
English: ...Because I'm in the number one spot?

Japanese: ウヌボレちゃダメッス。
English: Don't get all high and mighty on me here.

Japanese: ザンネンながら、アンタは ２位に転がり落ちたッス。
English: You've taken a tumble into second place, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥あれ、そうなんですか。 じゃあ、第１位は‥‥？
English: Huh? Really? Then who's in first...?

Japanese: 狩魔検事がブッちぎりで 独走してるッス。
English: Ms. von Karma's miles and miles ahead of you in this race.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: O-Oh...

Japanese: コワすぎるッス。 今の、か、狩魔検事は‥‥
English: She's really scary... Right now... M-Ms. von Karma... You really don't...

Japanese: （声がうわずっている‥‥）
English: (He's so scared of her that he's whispering now...)

Japanese: 自分はあまり 活劇にはくわしくないッスが‥‥
English: I'm not a real fan of action shows or anything...

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオと王都楼　真悟 ぐらいは知ってるッス。
English: But I know who Juan Corrida and Matt Engarde are.

Japanese: この２人、いつでも名前が ならんでいますよね。
English: You can't talk about one without talking about the other, I guess.

Japanese: なにぶん、デビューも 近かったし‥‥
English: They even debuted around the same time.

Japanese: おたがい、ものスゴい ライバル意識を持ってるッスから。
English: So they have this real fiery rivalry with each other.

Japanese: まるで、自分とアンタみたいに！
English: Kinda like you and me, pal!

Japanese: （イトノコさん‥‥ぼくに ライバル意識を持ってたんだ）
English: (I never knew he thought of me as a rival...)

Japanese: ‥‥アンタも、今度こそ ネングのおさめどきッス。
English: Hey, why don't we let bygones be bygones, pal?

Japanese: コイツは有罪ッス。 １００円かけてもいいッス。
English: Look, this guy is guilty. A buck says so.

Japanese: １００円‥‥って、春美ちゃんの おこづかいですよ？
English: One dollar...? Sure you're not talking about Pearls' allowance money?

Japanese: そ、それ以上はムリッス。 きゅ、給料が‥‥
English: I... I can't bet any more than that... My salary... It's...

Japanese: （なんと言われようとも‥‥）
English: (It doesn't matter what we think...)

Japanese: （ぼくは彼を 弁護しなければならないんだ！）
English: (I still have to defend Mr. Engarde in court or else!)

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Mr. Nick...

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟の マネージャーッスね。
English: She's Mr. Engarde's manager, right?

Japanese: ‥‥どうも、こういうタイプは ニガテッス。
English: No matter what I do, I really stink at talking with people like her.

Japanese: 話してると、なんとなく どぎまぎしちまうッス。
English: As soon as I open my mouth, I get all flustered.

Japanese: しかも彼女、アタマがイイッス。 まさに“デキるオンナ”ッス。
English: See, she's one of those women with a brain. She's someone who "gets things done".

Japanese: たしかに、そんな感じですね。 しっかりしたヒトだし。
English: Yeah, she does give that sort of impression, like she's got it all together.

Japanese: こっちの“デキないオトコ”ぶりが アザヤカにキワだって、
English: Compared to her, you can easily tell I'm a "can't do anything right" kind of guy.

Japanese: それはそれは やるせないキモチになるッス。
English: That's why I get all nervous and start messing up.

Japanese: （ちょっと、わかる気がする‥‥）
English: (I feel sort of bad for him...)

Japanese: どうですか‥‥これ。
English: What do you know about this?

Japanese: うむむ。特に情報は ないッスねえ。
English: Um... There's nothing special I can tell you about this, pal.

Japanese: 早く、もっと重要なものを 見せてほしいッス。
English: Look pal, we don't have a lot of time. Why don't you show me something more important?

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事にこんな トコロを見られたら、
English: If Ms. von Karma saw me here like this...

Japanese: おムコに行けなくなっちゃうッス。
English: I'd have to switch sides real fast...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: March 21 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: おや‥‥いらっしゃいませんね、 あの、おヒゲの刑事さん。
English: Hmm, it doesn't look like Mr. Scruffy Detective is here...

Japanese: （狩魔検事がヒゲヒゲ連呼する から、ウツったらしいな‥‥）
English: (Great, now even Pearls is calling him "Scruffy"... Thanks Franziska.)

Japanese: さっき、“捜査会議が始まる” って言ってたからね。
English: They said something about an investigation briefing earlier, right?

Japanese: ‥‥終わったころ もう一度、来てみようか。
English: Why don't we come back and try again later when they're done...

Japanese: はあ‥‥しかたないですね。
English: OK... I guess that's all we can do, right Mr. Nick?

Japanese: まあ、愛らしい。
English: Aww, it's kind of cute.

Japanese: なるほどくん。この お人形さんは‥‥？
English: Mr. Nick, what is this stuffed animal's name...?

Japanese: “タイホくん”だよ。
English: That's the "Blue Badger"!

Japanese: ワシが考えて、ワシが作った この署のマスコットさ。
English: It was my idea! I made it! It's this precinct's mascot, you know.

Japanese: そうなのですか‥‥
English: Oh, wow...

Japanese: コイツの人気を全国区にするのが、 ワシの最後の仕事だよ。
English: I'll get him assigned mascot of every police station, if it's the last thing I do!

Japanese: おしごと、がんばってください。
English: I hope you succeed in your mission, sir!

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “吐いちまえよ。 ラクになるぞ”
English: "...a break and spit it out, you lowlife scumbag..."

Japanese: “‥‥天丼、食うか？ 毎日カツ丼じゃ、あきるだろ？”
English: "...a turkey sandwich? Don'tcha eat ham every day? Get sick of it or something?"

Japanese: 取り調べのイメージ・トレーニング をしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for interrogations.

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: This must be the chief of the detectives here.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンの画面を、 食いいるように見つめている。
English: He's glued to his computer screen.

Japanese: なにぃ‥‥！　ヒーロー・オブ・ ヒーローの授賞式で‥‥
English: WHAT!? The Hero of Heroes award ceremony...

Japanese: “忍者ナンジャ、今年も賞を逃す” だと‥‥！
English: The grand prize got away from the Jammin' Ninja again!?

Japanese: （‥‥もっとダイジな事件が あるだろう！）
English: (Shouldn't you be reading something more important...?)

Japanese: 刑事たちの机がならんでいる。
English: These are the detectives' desks.

Japanese: パソコンに、資料。 ‥‥思ったより片づいているな。
English: There are computers and files on each one. Funny, they're a lot tidier than I expected.

Japanese: たぶん、あまり席にいることが ないんだろう。
English: I guess the detectives don't spend a lot of time at their desks.

Japanese: 婦人警官がビシッと 敬礼している。
English: A poster of a female police officer...

Japanese: ‥‥と思ったら、制服を着た アイドルのグラビアだった。
English: Wait, no. That's the latest "Babes in Uniform" calendar. My bad.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: March 21 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事‥‥捜査会議、 って言ってたけど‥‥。
English: Detective Gumshoe said they had an investigation briefing...

Japanese: そうですね。 ‥‥あ。帰ってきました！
English: Yeah... Oh! He's back!

Japanese: あ‥‥来ちゃった、ッスか。
English: Hey, so you came, pal.

Japanese: いきなりなんですか、 ‥‥そのアイサツ。
English: Why the blunt greeting?

Japanese: いや‥‥もう、 合わせるカオがないッス。
English: Um, because there's nothing to be friendly or happy about.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean by that?

Japanese: こんなときにかぎって‥‥ カンペキッス。証拠も、証言も。
English: Well, things look perfect this time around. The evidence and testimony are airtight...

Japanese: そんな‥‥ 負けるわけには‥‥いかないのに！
English: But... But... We can't just roll over and die! We have to stay positive!

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “警察だ！　‥‥よーし、全員 カベに向かって手をつくんだ”
English: "Freeze! Police! Everyone against the wall, hands where I can see 'em!"

Japanese: “コラ、イトノコ！　お前まで 手ェついてどうするんだよ！”
English: "Hey, what are you doing, Gumshoe!? Their hands, not yours!"

Japanese: 現場に踏み込むときのイメージ・ トレーニングをしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for arrests.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “しっかりしろ！　キズは たいしたコトねえんだ‥‥”
English: "Hang in there! You'll be OK, buddy! You're not injured that badly! A hang nail isn't..."

Japanese: “‥‥お、おい。イトノコ！ イトノコオオオオォォォォッ！”
English: "...H-Hey. Gumshoe! GUMSHOE! GUMSHOOOOOOOOOE!!"

Japanese: 仲間の死を看とるイメージ・ トレーニングをしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for when a fellow officer is injured.

Japanese: あの‥‥ カンペキな証拠‥‥って？
English: So what do you mean the evidence is airtight...?

Japanese: あまり詳しくは話せないッスが‥‥ ２つ、あるッス。
English: I can't give you all the details, pal, but there's two big pieces.

Japanese: ふ、ふたつも‥‥！
English: T-Two!?

Japanese: 両方とも、この現場写真に 写ってるッス。
English: And both of them are in this photo.

Japanese: ‥‥１つ目は、死体のムネから 消えたボタンッス。
English: The first is the button that's missing from the victim's chest.

Japanese: たしか‥‥なくなったボタンは、 身体検査のとき‥‥
English: Hmm... That's the button that you found during your body search of Mr. Engarde...

Japanese: そッス。トノサマンの衣装の ハカマから発見されたッス。
English: Yup. I found it in the folds of the Nickel Samurai's special pants.

Japanese: ううう‥‥ じゃあ、２つ目は‥‥？
English: Um... Uh... And the second one is...?

Japanese: ムネに刺さったナイフッス。 犯人の指紋が残っていたッス。
English: The knife in his chest, pal. The fingerprints on the knife in his chest, to be exact.

Japanese: しもん‥‥ あの。どなたの？
English: Fingerprints...? Um... Whose are they?

Japanese: 聞くまでもないッス。 ‥‥王都楼　真悟ッス。
English: You didn't even have to ask, little missy. It's obvious. They're Matt Engarde's.

Japanese: （明日の裁判‥‥ やっぱり、絶体絶命、か‥‥）
English: (Tomorrow's trial... Talk about being stuck between a rock and a hard place...)

Japanese: それで‥‥ カンペキな証言、というのは？
English: So what about this "airtight testimony"...?

Japanese: あの警備員のオバチャン、ッス。
English: It's that old security lady, Ms. Oldbag.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥
English: I thought so...

Japanese: “やっぱり”って‥‥ 聞いたッスか？　オバチャンから。
English: What do you mean, you thought so? Did she tell you some- thing, pal?

Japanese: ええ、まあ。
English: Um, well...

Japanese: ダレにも言わないよう、 口止めしといたのに‥‥。
English: And I even told her not to open that mouth of hers and blab to anyone...

Japanese: （オバチャンに、そいつは 不可能だろ‥‥）
English: (Her blab knob is stuck on ten, and there's no turning it down... Trust me.)

Japanese: オバチャンは見てたッス。
English: Yeah, well... Ms. Oldbag saw it all, pal.

Japanese: 死亡推定時刻のころ、殺人現場から 出てきた、王都楼　真悟を。
English: She saw Mr. Engarde come out of the victim's room around the estimated time of death.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥！
English: N-No way!

Japanese: 被害者のマネージャーで、 しかも華宮　霧緒のセンパイ。
English: She was the victim's manager and was also Ms. Adrian Andrews' mentor.

Japanese: その女性が自殺して、２年。 今度はそのふたりが交際している。
English: It's been two years since her suicide, and now those two are linked again by another death.

Japanese: あるいはグーゼンかも しれないッス。しかし‥‥？
English: Or maybe it's just a coincidence...? But...

Japanese: はがアァッ！
English: Waaah!

Japanese: ‥‥コリないバカに つきあうのは、もうコリゴリ。
English: I'm getting sick of dealing with one foolish idiot after another...

Japanese: かかか、狩魔検事ィッ！
English: M-M-Ms. von Karma!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやらキサマは 敵と味方の区別もつかないようね。
English: You can't seem to stop allying yourself with the enemy, can you?

Japanese: そんな刑事、 ここには必要ない。
English: I don't need a traitor in my midst.

Japanese: まま、まさか‥‥それは‥‥
English: Y-Y-You don't... You don't mean...

Japanese: ‥‥そう。クビよ。
English: I do. Scruffy.

Japanese: あなたはもう、必要ないわ。 ３０分以内に出ていきなさい。
English: You have thirty minutes to get out of here. You are no longer needed. Good bye.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥
English: Th-That's...

Japanese: ちょちょ、ちょっと待って ほしいッス！
English: W-Wait... Please wait, sir!

Japanese: 今月の給料がもらえないと自分は
English: If I don't get this month's pay, I'll star--

Japanese: うるさいッ！
English: Quiet!

Japanese: だいたい、キサマのような ウラギリ者さえいなければ‥‥
English: If it weren't for traitors like you...

Japanese: “私は負けなかった” ‥‥そう言いたいのか？
English: "I would've won." Is that what you want to say?

Japanese: ‥‥！　だ、だれッ！
English: Wh-Who!?

Japanese: ‥‥この声は‥‥
English: That voice...

Japanese: ‥‥ミツルギイイイィィィッ‥‥
English: E-Edgeworth!!

Japanese: ‥‥ひさしぶりだな‥‥成歩堂。
English: It's been a long time... Wright.

Japanese: こ、このかたが‥‥ みつるぎ検事‥‥
English: Th-This person... This is Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ‥‥。
English: What am I going to do with you...?

Japanese: なんでもヒトのせいにする、 その性格。
English: Still blaming others when things go wrong?

Japanese: ‥‥変わらないな、メイ。
English: You haven't changed a bit, Franziska.

Japanese: ‥‥よ‥‥よくも‥‥
English: Y... You...

Japanese: よく恥ずかしくもなく、 私の前にカオを出せたものね！
English: How dare you show your face to me without a shred of shame upon it!?

Japanese: 狩魔の名前にさんざん ドロをぬったうえ‥‥、
English: You've soiled the Von Karma name and dragged it through the mud.

Japanese: シッポを巻いて逃げたくせに！
English: Run away with your tail between your legs like the ill-bred dog you are!

Japanese: “狩魔は、カンペキをもって よしとする‥‥”か。
English: Are you talking about the Von Karma family creed? "To be perfect in every way..."

Japanese: それでは、キミはどうなんだ？ ‥‥メイ。
English: Then let's hear it, Franziska. How are things going?

Japanese: どうやら、この国では かなり苦戦しているようだが。
English: I hear you are having a rough time maintaining perfection in this country.

Japanese: く‥‥くッ！
English: Y-You!

Japanese: どうやら、キミには 荷が重いようだ。
English: You seem to be getting crushed under the weight of it all.

Japanese: ‥‥だからこうして、 帰ってきたのだよ。
English: That's why I came back.

Japanese: か、勝手なコトを言わないで！
English: Keep your assumptions to yourself!

Japanese: 私は‥‥私はまだ、 負けを認めたわけじゃない！
English: I... I haven't given in yet! I won't lose!

Japanese: この事件は私のもの。 ‥‥ゼッタイに、わたさない！
English: This case is mine! I'll never hand it over to you! Never!

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥明日。 法廷で会いましょう‥‥。
English: I will see you tomorrow... In court.

Japanese: どこかの負け犬さんに、“勝利”の イミを教えてあげないとね。
English: It will be a clinical lesson on the meaning of "total victory"!

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。あいかわらずの ジャジャ馬だな。
English: Hmph. Still the same wild mare she always was.

Japanese: ‥‥このゴシップは、 ワレワレもキョーミがあるッス。
English: We're pretty interested in this bit of gossip ourselves.

Japanese: イサオさんのスキャンダルに？ どうしてですか‥‥。
English: The scandal with Mr. Corrida? But why?

Japanese: 今から２年前‥‥
English: Well, two years ago...

Japanese: １人の女性が‥‥ 自殺しているッス。
English: A woman committed suicide.

Japanese: じさつ‥‥
English: Suicide...

Japanese: 彼女の名前は天野　由利恵。
English: Her name was Celeste Inpax.

Japanese: ‥‥藤見野　イサオの マネージャーをしていたッス。
English: And she was Juan Corrida's manager.

Japanese: 被害者の‥‥マネージャー‥‥
English: The victim's manager...?

Japanese: それだけじゃないッス。
English: But that's not all, pal.

Japanese: 天野　由利恵は、華宮　霧緒の 先輩マネージャーとして‥‥、
English: Ms. Inpax was Ms. Adrian Andrews' mentor...

Japanese: 彼女にシゴトをイチから たたきこんだ、いわば師匠ッス。
English: She taught Ms. Andrews everything she knew about the business from square one.

Japanese: おししょうさま‥‥
English: Her mentor...

Japanese: （イサオさんのマネージャーで、 霧緒さんのセンパイ‥‥）
English: (A woman who was both Mr. Corrida's manager and Ms. Andrews' mentor...)

Japanese: （その女性の自殺が‥‥ 何か事件に関係あるのか？）
English: (Could her suicide have something to do with this case...?)

Japanese: 彼女のコト‥‥ もっと、聞きたいッスか？
English: Do you want to know more about her, pal?

Japanese: このギターケースですけど‥‥。
English: What can you tell me about this guitar case...?

Japanese: ああ、それ。
English: Oh, that.

Japanese: 聞いた話ッスが、忍者ナンジャの トレードマークらしいッスね。
English: This is just what I heard, pal, but that's the Jammin' Ninja's signature item.

Japanese: ホテルのお部屋には、ギターは ありませんでしたけど‥‥。
English: But... The guitar wasn't in his hotel room...

Japanese: ワレワレも今、探してるッス。
English: Yeah, we looked for it too.

Japanese: 授賞式にトレードマークを 忘れるなんて、不自然ッスから。
English: It's not normal for a person to forget to bring their most famous item to an award show.

Japanese: （まっかなギター‥‥か。 カンケイあるのかな、事件に）
English: (It's starting to sound like that red guitar is related to this case after all...)

Japanese: グラスの中身は トマトジュースだったッス。
English: The stuff in that glass is tomato juice, pal.

Japanese: トマトジュース‥‥
English: Tomato juice...

Japanese: なんでも、被害者の 大好物だったらしいッス。
English: I heard the victim really loved that stuff. Favorite drink in the world.

Japanese: ‥‥大好物ですか‥‥。 （それは初耳だな‥‥）
English: ...It was his favorite drink? (First time I've heard that...)

Japanese: こう言っちゃあ なんスけど‥‥
English: You look at this photo and you can't help it.

Japanese: “まさにサツジンゲンバ” ‥‥って感じッスねえ。
English: It makes you want to say, "I can't believe this is the scene of a murder."

Japanese: （争ったあとに、 ムネに突き立ったナイフ‥‥）
English: (Stabbed with a knife after a struggle...)

Japanese: （この中に、きっと何か、 手がかりが眠っているはずだ）
English: (A clue is sleeping in this photo somewhere... I know it.)

Japanese: 自分はあまり 活劇にはくわしくないッスが‥‥
English: I'm not a real fan of action shows or anything...

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオといえば、 さすがに知ってるッス。
English: But I know who Juan Corrida and Matt Engarde are.

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟の人気をうばおうと 必死だったッス。
English: Juan Corrida, though, he was always desperately trying to be better than Matt Engarde.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタも、今度こそ ネングのおさめどきッス。
English: Hey, why don't we let bygones be bygones, pal?

Japanese: コイツは有罪ッス。 １００円かけてもいいッス。
English: Look, this guy is guilty. A buck says so.

Japanese: １００円‥‥って、春美ちゃんの おこづかいですよ？
English: One dollar...? Sure you're not talking about Pearls' allowance money?

Japanese: そ、それ以上はムリッス。 きゅ、給料が‥‥
English: I... I can't bet any more than that... My salary... It's...

Japanese: （なんと言われようとも‥‥）
English: (It doesn't matter what we think...)

Japanese: （ぼくは彼を 弁護しなければならないんだ！）
English: (I still have to defend Mr. Engarde in court or else!)

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Mr. Nick...

Japanese: 彼女については、ちょっと 気になるコトがあるッス。
English: There's something interesting about this woman, pal.

Japanese: なんですか？
English: And what is that?

Japanese: 知らないッスか？ 彼女のウワサ。
English: You don't know? Haven't you heard the gossip about her?

Japanese: （ウワサ‥‥ねえ‥‥）
English: (Gossip, huh...)

Japanese: （あの記事のこと、聞いてみるか）
English: (I wonder if he knows anything about that article...)

Japanese: ‥‥刑事課で情報を 流しているのを見つかったら、
English: If I'm caught leaking info to you here in the precinct,

Japanese: クビがあぶないッス。
English: my neck's gonna be in a ringer, pal.

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥クビ、ですか！
English: Y-Your neck!? No!

Japanese: いやいや、さすがに クビは冗談ッスケド‥‥
English: Ah, I mean my "neck" as a joke...

Japanese: 事件にカンケイのあるコトだけ、 聞いてほしいッス。
English: But yeah, ask me about things that have to do with the case only, alright pal?

Japanese: ‥‥いろいろあったが、 検事・狩魔　豪は私の師匠だった。
English: A lot of things may have happened, however Manfred von Karma was still my mentor.

Japanese: “カンペキな勝利” ‥‥それが、狩魔の証なのだ。
English: And a "perfect win record" is proof of a Von Karma.
...One year ago,

Japanese: いくつかの事件で‥‥お前は 被告の有罪を立証できなかった。
English: you could not establish guilt in a few cases...

Japanese: 突然、検事局を去ったのは そのせいなんだろう？
English: Are those losses the reason you suddenly disappeared from the Prosecutor's Office?

Japanese: お前の大事な“カンペキな勝利”が 無に帰したワケだからな。
English: Did you leave because you had lost your "perfect win record"...?

Japanese: そんな自分勝手な理由で 法廷に立つ検事なんて‥‥
English: To think your motivation for prosecuting trials was so selfish...

Japanese: 生き返ってこなければ よかったんだよ、御剣！
English: It'd been better for everyone if you never came back from the dead, Edgeworth!

Japanese: それならば‥‥ 聞いてみたいものだな。
English: I see. Then let me ask you something.

Japanese: キミはなぜ、法廷に立つ？ ‥‥その理由は、なんだ。
English: Why do YOU stand in the courtroom? What is your reason?

Japanese: 狩魔　冥‥‥。ぼくを 見るとかならず、こう言う。
English: Well, if it was Franziska, she would almost definitely say,

Japanese: “今度こそ、キサマを倒す！”
English: "I will defeat you this time!" the instant she saw me.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: ‥‥法廷は、弁護士と 検事が戦う場所じゃない。
English: The courtroom is not a personal battlefield for prosecutors and lawyers.

Japanese: ぼくは、依頼人のために 法廷に立っているつもりだ。
English: I stand in the courtroom to defend my client.

Japanese: ‥‥彼らを、救うために。
English: ...To save their lives.

Japanese: 被告人を‥‥“救う”‥‥だと？
English: To save your client, you say?

Japanese: 自分が勝訴することしか 考えていない‥‥
English: Those who think only of their own ego-driven goals...

Japanese: そんな検事、ぼくは認めない。
English: Those kind of prosecutors are reprehensible to me.

Japanese: たとえそいつが“天才” と呼ばれていようと。
English: Even if you're a "prodigy"...

Japanese: ‥‥お前のことだよ、御剣。
English: Or someone like you, Edgeworth...

Japanese: どうやらキミは、 大きな誤解をしているようだ。
English: It looks like there is still a lot you have yet to learn.

Japanese: ご、ゴカイ‥‥だって？
English: "A lot I have yet to learn"? Me...?
...Hmph.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、いい。 今にわかるときが来るだろう‥‥
English: Well, that's enough for now. The time when you will see is coming soon enough...

Japanese: ‥‥死んだはずじゃなかったのか？ “検事・御剣　怜侍”は‥‥
English: I thought you, the Prosecutor Miles Edgeworth, had gone and died!

Japanese: な、なるほどくん‥‥！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: お前のカオなんて‥‥ 二度と見たくなかったよ。
English: I... I never wanted to see you again!

Japanese: ‥‥ずいぶんなアイサツだな。 １年ぶりにしては‥‥。
English: I think that's enough of a "warm welcome" for someone you haven't seen in a year...

Japanese: 明日の法廷‥‥ 出てくるつもりなのか？
English: Are you going to run tomorrow's trial...?

Japanese: ‥‥キミも聞いただろう？
English: ...You heard her, right?

Japanese: あのジャジャ馬はまだ、 負けを認めないつもりらしい。
English: That wild mare hasn't given in yet, it seems.

Japanese: 私の出番はないだろう。
English: So, no, I don't think I'll be making an appearance.

Japanese: ‥‥キミが私をニクむのは勝手だ。
English: Your hatred for me is quite unhealthy. Not to mention one-sided.

Japanese: ただ‥‥、ひとつだけ 言えることがある。
English: But I will say one thing...

Japanese: 明日の法廷‥‥キミひとりの チカラでは、勝つことはできない。
English: You can't win on your own at the trial tomorrow.

Japanese: （‥‥どういうことだ‥‥？）
English: (What is that supposed to mean...?)

Japanese: 私にはあって、キミには 決定的に欠けているものがある。
English: I have something definitive that you lack.

Japanese: ‥‥それは、組織だ。 ジジツを探りだすチカラ。
English: And that's the definition of teamwork. It's the power to find the truth.
"The truth"...?

Japanese: この事件を理解するためには、 ある“ジジツ”を知る必要がある。
English: In order to understand this case, you have to understand a certain "truth".

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、その気になったら、 声をかけたまえ。
English: ...Well, if you ever feel the need for my assistance, it is available to you.

Japanese: 私はこの事件の担当検事ではない。 ‥‥情報をくれてやっても、いい。
English: I'm not in charge of this case, so I can be a bit more generous with information.

Japanese: （いったいコイツ‥‥ 何を考えているんだ‥‥？）
English: (Just what is going on inside his head...?)

Japanese: 彼女の死は、たしかに 自殺だった。それはまちがいない。
English: Ms. Inpax's death was most certainly a suicide. Of that there is no mistake.

Japanese: しかし‥‥その遺書は、 どこからも発見されていない。
English: However, we could not find her suicide note.

Japanese: ‥‥警察では、だれかが かくした、と考えている。
English: That's when the police began to suspect that someone had hidden it.

Japanese: 遺書を‥‥？
English: The suicide note?

Japanese: でも、そもそも由利恵さんが そんなものを書いた証拠は‥‥？
English: But how do you know Ms. Inpax had even written such a note?

Japanese: たしかに、証拠はない。 しかし‥‥、
English: There was no solid evidence, however...

Japanese: 彼女の右手の指先から、 インクの跡が検出されている。
English: We did find traces of ink on her right index finger.

Japanese: ‥‥彼女が遺書を残した カクリツは、きわめて高いのだ。
English: Which makes the likelihood of a suicide note very high.

Japanese: だけど‥‥かくした、って いったい‥‥だれが？
English: But who would hide such a thing...?

Japanese: 警察では、藤見野　イサオ氏だと 考えている。
English: The police think it was Mr. Juan Corrida himself.

Japanese: ひ、被害者の‥‥？
English: Th-The victim...?

Japanese: 彼女の死体の発見者は、 藤見野　イサオだったのだ。
English: He was the one who found her body.

Japanese: 遺書をかくすチャンスが あったのは‥‥彼しかいない。
English: Which makes him the only person who had a chance to hide her suicide note.

Japanese: （藤見野　イサオが、自分の マネージャーの遺書を‥‥？）
English: (Mr. Corrida hid his own manager's suicide note...? But why...?)

Japanese: ‥‥遺書が発見されないかぎり、 これ以上の推理は無意味だが‥‥
English: As long as her note is missing, any speculation beyond this is meaningless...

Japanese: とりあえず、こいつに 目を通しておくといい。
English: For now, I think you should look this over.

Japanese: 自殺の報告書 ‥‥その、１本目だ。
English: This is the suicide report... Part one anyway...
(Part one...?)

Japanese: 証拠品《自殺の報告書》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Suicide Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: “華宮　霧緒”と“依存” ‥‥どうつながるんだ？
English: So, how are "Adrian Andrews" and "co-dependency" related?

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒が自殺を こころみたのは‥‥
English: Adrian Andrews' attempt at suicide...

Japanese: 天野　由利恵が亡くなった 数日後のことだった。
English: was a few days after the death of Celeste Inpax.

Japanese: それが‥‥？
English: And...?

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒がなぜ、 自殺を考えたか‥‥？
English: And why did Adrian Andrews think about committing suicide...?

Japanese: “生きていく自信を失ったから” ‥‥なのだそうだ。
English: Quite possibly because she had "lost her will to live."

Japanese: ‥‥自信を‥‥？ いったい、どうして‥‥
English: Lost her will...? But why would she...?

Japanese: 信じきっていた先輩、 天野　由利恵がいなくなったから。
English: Her pillar of strength, her mentor Celeste Inpax, was gone forever. That's why.

Japanese: な、なんだって‥‥
English: Wh-Why would that...?

Japanese: それってつまり‥‥ “あとおいじさつ”‥‥ですか？
English: Is this what they call, "following someone to the grave"...?

Japanese: ‥‥自殺未遂後、華宮　霧緒は カウンセラーの面談を受けている。
English: After her attempted suicide, Adrian Andrews started attending counseling sessions.

Japanese: 彼女は‥‥自分がココロから 信頼できる人間を見つけて、
English: She is a person who looks for someone she can trust unconditionally.

Japanese: その人物に、盲目的に したがおうとする‥‥
English: And once she finds that someone, she blindly follows them.

Japanese: そうでなければ、不安で 生きていくことができないそうだ。
English: Without someone to guide her, she feels uneasy and can't carry herself through life.

Japanese: ‥‥それが‥‥ 彼女の“依存”なのか‥‥？
English: And that's... That's her "co-dependency"...?

Japanese: 天野　由利恵に 突然、自殺されてしまって‥‥
English: When Celeste Inpax suddenly committed suicide,

Japanese: 目の前が真っ暗になった ‥‥華宮　霧緒はそう語っている。
English: the world before her turned pitch dark... That's according to Adrian Andrews herself.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥ あの、自信に満ちたタイドは‥‥
English: Then... That means her super-confident attitude...

Japanese: すべて‥‥ウソ、だ。
English: It's all a façade.

Japanese: 天野　由利恵にしたがって、 そのようにふるまっているだけだ。
English: She's only copying her mentor's behavior to hold herself together.

Japanese: （‥‥なんてこった‥‥）
English: (How terrible...)

Japanese: 証拠品《自殺未遂の報告書》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Attempted Suicide Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥つまらない証拠について 語り合うシュミはない。
English: I have no interest in talking about useless evidence.

Japanese: もっと、考えるべきだな、 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: Put a little more thought into what you show me... Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: （やっぱり、キザなヤツ‥‥）
English: (Still as stuck up as ever...)

Japanese: ‥‥報告書に目を通すのは 好きではない。
English: I don't like to look through reports.

Japanese: それが自殺の報告書であれば、 なおさらだ。
English: I like suicide reports even less.

Japanese: さらに、それが２本いっぺんとも なれば、サイアク‥‥だな。
English: Worst of all are the reports that have multiple parts like that one. That has two.
...Two parts?

Japanese: コイツが、２本目の 報告書だ。
English: What you just handed me is the first part of the report... Here is the second part.

Japanese: （こっちは‥‥ 自殺未遂の報告書か‥‥）
English: (The second part of the report is about an attempted suicide...)

Japanese: 自殺者の氏名は ‥‥“華宮　霧緒”！
English: The attempter's name...! It's "Adrian Andrews"!

Japanese: き、きりおさんというのは ‥‥あ、あの？
English: M-Ms. Andrews...? Um, what did she do?

Japanese: 彼女が‥‥じ、 自殺未遂だって‥‥？
English: She... She tried to kill herself...?

Japanese: とてもそんなコト、 するような人には見えなかった‥‥
English: She doesn't seem like the kind of person to try and kill herself...

Japanese: さっそうとしたキャリア・ウーマン ‥‥それが、彼女のイメージだ。
English: You think she's a strong career woman? That is just her image.

Japanese: しかし‥‥華宮　霧緒には、 あるヒミツがあったのだ。
English: Adrian Andrews... She has a certain secret she's always trying to hide.

Japanese: ヒミツが‥‥？
English: A secret...?

Japanese: ‥‥“依存”。 それが、彼女のキーワードだ。
English: ...Her "co-dependency". That's the key word.

Japanese: （“いぞん”‥‥彼女にイチバン 似合わないコトバだぞ‥‥）
English: ("Co-dependency"... The word most unsuited to describing that woman...)

Japanese: ‥‥人は見かけによらない‥‥
English: Appearances can be deceiving.

Japanese: 月並みな言いかただが ‥‥まさに、そのとおりだな。
English: It's such a cliché saying... But it's cliché because it's true.

Japanese: 霧緒さん‥‥。
English: Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: （あの、シッカリした タイドのウラに‥‥）
English: (To think that behind that unwavering, brave front...)

Japanese: （そんな弱さが かくされていたなんて‥‥）
English: (She's been hiding this weakness in fear...)

Japanese: ‥‥彼については、 気になるウワサがあるな。
English: There is an interesting rumor about this man...

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒が、 彼に接近していた、ってヤツか？
English: You mean the one about Ms. Andrews getting close to him?

Japanese: でも‥‥週刊誌のゴシップだろ？
English: But that's pretty common tabloid fare, isn't it?

Japanese: ‥‥私なら、それだけで かたづける気はしないが‥‥。
English: I don't take things at face value when there's more to be found...

Japanese: しばらくこの国を はなれているうちに、
English: While I was abroad,

Japanese: こんな情けないタイプの 俳優が人気を集めるとはな。
English: these deplorable types of actors became popular, I take it.

Japanese: “春風のようにサワヤカ” がキャッチフレーズらしい。
English: Well, "refreshing like a spring breeze" is his motto...

Japanese: ‥‥ハッ。春風の どこがサワヤカなものか！
English: "R-Refreshing"!? And what is so refreshing about a spring breeze!?

Japanese: （どうやら、花粉症らしいな ‥‥この男）
English: (Sounds like the pollen is not treating him well this year...)

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒、か‥‥。
English: Adrian Andrews...

Japanese: ‥‥彼女には、 大きなヒミツがある。
English: She holds a large secret within herself.
A secret...?

Japanese: この事件のポイントは‥‥ 彼女にある、そう考えるべきだな。
English: You can't help but feel that this whole case revolves around her...

Japanese: ‥‥そう。彼女こそ、 この事件のカギをにぎる人物だ。
English: Hmm... This woman is another key to the case.

Japanese: そ、そうなのか‥‥？
English: D-Do you really think so?

Japanese: もともと彼女は、華宮　霧緒の 先輩だったのだが‥‥、
English: She was Adrian Andrews' mentor a long time ago,

Japanese: 突然プロダクションをうつり、 藤見野　イサオの担当になった。
English: but suddenly, she was called away by a production, and became Juan Corrida's manager.

Japanese: そして、その数ヶ月後‥‥ 天野　由利恵は死んだ。
English: And then, a few months later... Celeste Inpax died.

Japanese: で、でも‥‥彼女の死は、 自殺‥‥なんだろう？
English: B-But her death was a suicide, right?

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Yes.

Japanese: しかし、その自殺には、 ひとつ、大きなナゾがあるのだ。
English: But there is still one riddle left unsolved...
A riddle...?

Japanese: ‥‥遺書だ。
English: Her suicide note.

Japanese: 発見されなかったのだ。 ‥‥どこからも、な。
English: It went missing. No one could find it.

Japanese: （消えた遺書、か‥‥）
English: (A suicide note that just vanished, huh...)

Japanese: ‥‥我々に求められて いるのは、
English: We are looking into leads,

Japanese: 事件のキーポイントを 見抜く、たしかな眼力だ。
English: but we can only look into a few key players with our limited resources.

Japanese: ‥‥その人物は、 語るべきイミを持たない。
English: There's no reason for us to waste our energy investigating this person.

Japanese: （“情報はありません”って スナオに言えよ‥‥）
English: (Why don't you just tell it to me straight and say, "I don't have any info"?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: March 21 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: ‥‥いらっしゃいませんね、 みなさん。
English: There's no one here. Everyone's gone.

Japanese: ３人でなかよく ごはんを食べに行かれたとか。
English: I wonder if the three of them went to eat together!

Japanese: ‥‥それだけはないだろうね。 会議じゃないかな。また。
English: ...I don't think they'd ever do that. They're probably in yet another meeting.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “キホンは、カレーパン。 オニギリはやめたほうがいい”
English: "Can't dig through the cereal box..."

Japanese: “のりをまいているあいだに、 ターゲットを見落とすからな”
English: "Can't dig for the prize... Will lose sight of the target and ruin the cereal..."

Japanese: 張り込みするときのイメージ・ トレーニングをしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for a stake-out.

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: March 21 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: あの！　どうですか？
English: So! What now?

Japanese: ハッキリしたことがあるよ。 霧緒さんには、動機がある。
English: Well, we did find one thing out for sure. Ms. Andrews has a motive.

Japanese: 天野　由利恵さんの遺書、 ですか‥‥？
English: You mean Ms. Inpax's suicide note...?

Japanese: そう。それに彼女は、 第１発見者だ。細工だって‥‥
English: That's right. She was also the one to discover the victim's body. Clever...

Japanese: ‥‥ピピピピピピ‥‥
English: ...*beep beep beep*...

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！　なるほどくん！ トランシーバーが！
English: Ah! Mr. Nick! The transceiver!

Japanese: もしもし！　こちら、 成歩堂法律事務所ですが！
English: Hello!? This is the law office of Wright & Co!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士さん。 これは電話ではありませんよ。
English: ...Mr. Attorney, you're not answering a phone.

Japanese: ま、真宵ちゃんは‥‥！
English: M-Maya! Where's Maya!?

Japanese: ヤクソクどおり、 近づきすらしていません。
English: As I promised, I have not come within a few feet of her this whole time.

Japanese: よかった‥‥
English: Phew...

Japanese: ‥‥だから、ずいぶん おなかがへっていることでしょう。
English: ...Which is why, I suppose, she is absolutely famished.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: とにかく、早く無罪判決を 勝ちとることです。
English: So I suggest you win a quick acquittal, my friend.

Japanese: ‥‥どんなことをしてでも、ね。
English: At any cost. Wouldn't you agree?

Japanese: 待て！　真宵ちゃん！ 真宵ちゃんを‥‥
English: Wait! Maya! Let me hear her!

Japanese: しかたありませんね‥‥
English: ...Very well.

Japanese: ‥‥ちゃんに！
English: ...Ask my...!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんか！
English: Maya! Is that you!?

Japanese: ‥‥お姉ちゃん‥‥ お姉ちゃんに聞いてッ！
English: ...Sis... Ask my sis!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！　真宵ちゃん！ ‥‥くそッ、切れた！
English: Maya! Maya! ...Dammit! He cut me off!

Japanese: “お姉ちゃんに聞いて” ‥‥そう、おっしゃいましたね。
English: Mystic Maya said, "Ask my sis," didn't she?

Japanese: “お姉ちゃん”‥‥？ どういうことだろ。
English: "Sis"...? What does she mean by that...?

Japanese: わからないの？ ‥‥はあ‥‥。なさけないわね。
English: Come on, Phoenix! *sigh* You're a hopeless one...

Japanese: う。ご、ごめん‥‥ ‥‥あ‥‥！
English: Um, s-sorry... ...Ack!!

Japanese: 千尋さん！
English: Mia!

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。なんでも聞いて。 真宵からのメッセージもあるわ。
English: I have a message from Maya, so come, ask me anything you want about her.

Japanese: ‥‥真宵は今、暗いところに 閉じこめられているわ。
English: Maya is locked up in a very dark place right now.

Japanese: ‥‥聞かせてあげるわ。 真宵になったつもりで聞いて。
English: I'll tell you everything I heard when I was with her.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんに‥‥
English: When you were with her...

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃんのことが 気になる！
English: What's going on with Maya is the most important thing right now!

Japanese: 今は、千尋さんの話を 聞こう‥‥。
English: I should talk with Mia...

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは‥‥！
English: How's Maya!?

Japanese: ブジよ。‥‥今のところは。
English: She's safe... for now.

Japanese: ‥‥あのユーカイ犯、 ヤクソクは守るみたいね。
English: That kidnapper is one to keep his word, it seems.

Japanese: ‥‥よかった‥‥。 でも、千尋さんがどうして？
English: I'm glad to hear she's safe... But Mia, how did you know...?

Japanese: あの子、閉じこめられてすぐ、 私を呼びだしたの。
English: As soon as she was locked up, Maya called for me.

Japanese: 私は、あの子の書き残した メッセージを読んで‥‥、
English: I read the note she left...

Japanese: できるかぎりの情報を 集めてきた、ってわけ。
English: Then I gathered as much information about her surroundings as I could.

Japanese: （霊媒って‥‥こういう 使いかたもできるのか‥‥）
English: (I didn't know you could use spirit channeling like this... Pretty smart of her...)

Japanese: ユーカイ犯は！ どんなヤツなんですか！
English: The kidnapper! What's he like!?

Japanese: それは‥‥わからないわね。
English: I don't know...

Japanese: ホテルで電話に呼び出されて‥‥ 睡眠薬をかがされたらしいの。
English: Apparently, Maya went to answer a phone call at the hotel and was drugged there...

Japanese: それで‥‥！
English: And!?

Japanese: あの子、カオも 見ていないそうよ。
English: She didn't see the face of her attacker.

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Argh...

Japanese: ‥‥悪いけど、あなたの 話を聞いている時間はないわ。
English: Sorry, but I don't have time to answer your questions.

Japanese: （それもそうだな。 今は、真宵ちゃんのことを‥‥）
English: (Yeah... And we should be worrying about Maya right now...)
Date: ???  Time: ??? Location: ???

Japanese: うう‥‥はらぺこだあ。
English: Nnngh... I'm starving...

Japanese: ‥‥あれ食べたいな‥‥たいやき。
English: I could really go for some apple pie...

Japanese: やっぱり、こんなときは アマいものだよね‥‥って！
English: I mean, at a time like this, sweets are the only way to go!

Japanese: 殺さないってヤクソクしたのに！ 死んじゃうじゃないよ！
English: I have to stay positive! He promised he wasn't going to kill me! I'm not going to die!

Japanese: （お姉ちゃん‥‥なるほどくんに 会えたかなあ‥‥）
English: (Sis... I wonder if you're with Nick right now...)

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ごちゃごちゃ つみあげられてるけど‥‥
English: There's all sorts of things piled up here...

Japanese: 暗くてよく見えないな。
English: But it's too dark to see.

Japanese: なんだろ、これ。 ビンがいっぱいあるよ。
English: What's this...? It feels like there are a lot of glass bottles here.

Japanese: それに‥‥タルだねこりゃ。
English: And these... They feel like barrels.

Japanese: うう‥‥おなかすいたし、 いただいちゃおうかなあ。
English: I'll pass... Too bad I'm really hungry, and not really thirsty.

Japanese: やっぱり、 カギかかってるなあ‥‥。
English: Drat, it's locked.

Japanese: （あれ。でもこのカギ‥‥ なんか、カンタンにあきそう）
English: (Hmm, but this door's lock seems easy enough to open...)

Japanese: こんなとき、テレビだと、 カタいカードなんか使って‥‥
English: On TV, the hero always uses a plastic card or a stiff piece of cardboard...

Japanese: カチッ、て開けちゃうんだけど。
English: ...Then "click", they magically open the door.

Japanese: （どこか、落ちてないかな。 カード‥‥）
English: (I wonder if there's a card like that around here I could use...)

Japanese: あれ。‥‥こんなところに カードが落ちてるよ‥‥
English: Huh? Someone dropped a card here...

Japanese: 名刺‥‥みたいだけど、 名前が書いてないなあ。
English: It kinda looks like... a business card... But there's no name on it.

Japanese: これって‥‥サザエ、かな。
English: Hmm... It's a picture of a seashell, I think...

Japanese: ‥‥ヘンなカードだね、こりゃ。
English: What a strange card.

Japanese: あ、そうだ！ ‥‥このサザエのカード‥‥
English: Ah! That's it! This shell card!

Japanese: これを使えば、開けられるかも。 ‥‥ドアのカギ！
English: If I use this, maybe I can get the door open! This might be my key out of here!

Japanese: いやー、前からウスウス 気がついていたけど‥‥
English: I had a feeling this card might be useful...

Japanese: やっぱり、あたしって テンサイだよね！
English: I'm such a genius!

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、シツレイして‥‥
English: Alright. Now if you'll excuse me, Mr. Kidnapper...

Japanese: ‥カチ‥

English: (I did it!)

Japanese: ‥‥よ、よおし‥‥ じゃ、行ってみよう！
English: OK... Now I'm getting the heck out of here!

Japanese: （なるほどくんなんて、 待ってられないもん‥‥）
English: (I shouldn't keep Nick waiting... Or worried.)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: ‥‥あのドアをなんとかしないと、 どこにも行けないよね‥‥。
English: If I don't do something to the door first, I don't think I'll be "moving" anywhere...

Japanese: 暗くてよく見えないけど、 何もなさそうだね‥‥。
English: I can't see very well in this darkness, but it doesn't seem like there's anything there...
